description: The act of formally terminating credit card accounts with the issuing financial institution, which can affect total credit limit and utilization.

name: LEAVE CREDIT CARDS OPEN
description: The practice of maintaining active credit card accounts, even if not regularly used, to preserve or increase total available credit and improve credit utilization.

name: PAYING OFF CREDIT CARD BALANCE ON TIME
description: The practice of remitting the full outstanding amount owed on a credit card by the due date each month to avoid interest and build good credit.

name: TEXT ALERT REMINDERS
description: Short Message Service (SMS) notifications used to remind individuals about upcoming bill due dates.

name: SUBMITTING A DISPUTE
description: The act of formally challenging inaccurate or incomplete information on a credit report with the relevant credit reporting company and data furnisher.

name: REPAYING FEDERAL STUDENT LOANS
description: The process of making scheduled payments on federal student loans, with various options available to make them more affordable.

name: GOOD STANDING
description: The status of a loan when payments are made on time, which is important for qualifying for alternative repayment plans and avoiding negative credit impact.

name: PAYMENT SCHEDULE
description: The structured timeline detailing when and how much a borrower is required to pay towards a loan, under which payments must continue until an alternative is approved.

name: BORROWER PROTECTIONS
description: Safeguards or benefits provided to borrowers, which are generally more limited for private student loans compared to federal student loans.

name: REPAYING PRIVATE STUDENT LOANS
description: The process of making scheduled payments on private student loans, with generally more limited options than for federal loans, requiring communication with the loan servicer in case of difficulty.

name: UNPAID STUDENT LOANS
description: Student loans for which payments are not being made, leading to specific collection methods for federal loans and regular debt collection practices for private loans.

name: DEBT COLLECTION PRACTICES
description: The methods used by debt collectors to recover unpaid debts, which differ for federal and private student loans.

name: EXEMPT FEDERAL BENEFITS
description: Federal benefits that cannot be garnished by debt collectors or private student loan collectors, distinguishing them from other federal benefits that the Department of Education can garnish.

name: CONSUMERFINANCE.GOV/PAYING-FOR-COLLEGE
description: A specific section of the CFPB's website dedicated to helping people plan for education and training after high school, offering tools and resources.

name: ELECTRONIC PAY
description: A method of payment where wages are transferred electronically, such as via direct deposit or payroll card, as opposed to a paper check.

name: CARD BALANCE
description: The current amount of money available on a prepaid card, which users are advised to check to avoid decline fees.

name: TRANSACTION FEES
description: Charges applied per individual transaction made using a card, which can be an alternative to a monthly fee plan.

name: MONTHLY FEE PLAN
description: A payment structure for card usage where a single fixed fee is charged monthly, regardless of the number of transactions, as an alternative to a pay-as-you-go plan with transaction fees.

name: DISPUTED AMOUNT
description: The specific sum of money involved in an unauthorized transaction or error, which a card provider may be required to credit back to an account during an investigation under federal law.

name: ADDITIONAL FEATURES, SERVICES, OR ADD-ONS
description: Optional items that can be purchased with a car, such as extended warranties, upgraded packages, or service plans, increasing the total cost.

name: LOSS OF INCOME
description: The financial danger posed by reduced work hours or temporary unemployment, making it difficult to cover living expenses and bills.

name: FINANCIAL SERVICE FEES
description: Charges levied by financial institutions for various services, including account maintenance, ATM usage, overdrafts, or check cashing.

name: PREMIUM PAYMENT
description: The amount of money an individual or business pays for an insurance policy, typically on a recurring basis.

name: PHONE SERVICE
description: A telecommunications utility that provides mobile or landline connectivity, often with different rate plans.

name: LIFELINESUPPORT.ORG
description: The official website that provides information and helps determine eligibility for the Lifeline phone rate program.

name: PREPAID OR FIXED-RATE PHONE PLANS
description: Phone service plans where payment is made in advance or at a consistent rate, offering predictable costs.

name: TV, INTERNET, STREAMING SERVICES
description: A suite of digital entertainment and connectivity services provided by various companies, often available in different packages and price tiers.

name: PRINCIPAL BALANCE
description: The outstanding amount of a loan or debt, excluding interest and other charges, from which a percentage is calculated for minimum payments on the credit card account.

name: DEBT REPAYMENT DURATION
description: The estimated length of time it will take to pay off a specific debt, calculated as 17 years and 9 months for the example credit card debt if only minimum payments are made.

name: FIRM OFFER OF CREDIT OR INSURANCE
description: A pre-approved proposal for credit or insurance extended by companies after receiving limited information about a consumer, without accessing their full credit report.

name: LONG DEBT REPAYMENT PERIOD
description: The financial risk associated with an extended time frame to pay off a debt (e.g., 17 years and 9 months), resulting in significantly higher overall interest costs.

name: ACCOUNT REVIEW
description: An evaluation performed by a lender or company on an existing credit account or business transaction, which typically involves obtaining information from a consumer report without affecting credit scores.

name: PAYING DOWN A BALANCE
description: The process of making payments to reduce the outstanding amount owed on a credit account, an action taken by the individual consumer in the provided example.

name: REQUESTING ANNUAL CREDIT REPORT
description: The act of a consumer obtaining their own yearly credit report, which is considered a soft inquiry and does not affect credit scores.

name: LIQUIDATE ASSETS
description: The process of converting a consumer's property into cash to pay off creditors, a key component of Chapter 7 bankruptcy proceedings.

name: DISCHARGE DEBTS
description: The legal action of releasing a person from the obligation to pay certain debts, often as part of bankruptcy proceedings like Chapter 7.

name: REORGANIZE FINANCES
description: The process of restructuring one's financial obligations and payment plan, typically under a legal framework such as Chapter 13 bankruptcy.

name: REPAY DEBTS
description: The action of making payments to extinguish financial obligations, a fundamental aspect of managing credit and a requirement in processes like Chapter 13 bankruptcy.

name: STUDENTAID.ED.GOV/REPAY-LOANS
description: A government website providing further information about repaying student loans.

name: PRIVATE LOANS
description: Loans offered by private lenders, distinct from federal government loans.

name: PRIVATE LOAN SERVICER
description: A company that directly manages private student loans.

name: HOUSEHOLD INCOME
description: The total income of all members of a household, used to determine eligibility for certain repayment plans and affordable payments.

name: FAMILY SIZE
description: The number of people in a borrower's family, used along with income to determine income-driven repayment amounts.

name: PAYBACK STRATEGY
description: A method or approach chosen by a borrower to repay their debts, such as paying off smallest debts first or highest interest rate debts first.

name: SMALLEST DEBT
description: Refers to a specific debt with the lowest outstanding balance among a borrower's multiple debts, often targeted in certain payback strategies.

name: HIGHEST INTEREST RATE
description: A characteristic of a debt indicating the highest percentage charge for borrowing money, often prioritized in certain payback strategies.

name: ATLANTA, GEORGIA
description: The city and state where Equifax's P.O. Box for security freeze requests is located.

name: ALLEN, TX
description: The city and state where Experian's P.O. Box for security freeze requests is located.

name: CHESTER, PA
description: The city and state where Transunion's P.O. Box for credit freeze requests is located.

name: RED FLAGS
description: Warning signs indicating potentially unfavorable or fraudulent financial products or services that consumers should be wary of.

name: PRESSURED SALES TACTICS
description: A red flag where consumers are coerced into purchases or loans they may not want or afford.

name: UNEXPLAINED FEES
description: A red flag where the charges associated with a financial product or service lack clear justification.

name: NO CLEAR CANCELLATION OR RETURN POLICY
description: A red flag indicating a lack of transparency regarding the terms for reversing a purchase or service.

name: INCONSISTENT INFORMATION ON INTEREST RATES
description: A red flag where the verbally quoted interest rate differs from the written documentation.

name: HIGH COST LOAN
description: A loan characterized by higher than average interest rates or fees, often a result of predatory lending practices.

name: REGULAR PRIME LOAN
description: A standard loan offered to borrowers with good credit, typically at favorable terms.

name: PAPERWORK DOESN'T MATCH SALES PITCH
description: A red flag where written contract terms contradict verbal promises made by a salesperson.

name: CONFUSING FINE-PRINT
description: A red flag where the detailed clauses of a contract are intentionally or unintentionally difficult to understand, leading to a rule to refuse to sign anything not understood.

name: ADDITIONAL INSURANCE AND OTHER ADD-ON PRODUCTS
description: Unnecessary or optional items like warranties or monitoring services that lenders may push borrowers to purchase, increasing the total loan cost.

name: YARD WORK
description: The act of performing maintenance or tasks in a garden or yard, often a service that can be offered for a fee to generate income.

name: TALENTS OR HOBBIES
description: Personal skills or leisure activities that can be leveraged and monetized to generate additional income.

name: HOUSE CLEANING
description: The act of cleaning a house, a service that can be traded for other services or provided for a fee.

name: BABY-SITTING
description: The act of caring for a child, a service that can be traded for other services or provided for a fee.

name: CURRENT JOB
description: An individual's existing employment position, which is a primary source of income and where opportunities for raises or additional hours might exist.

name: JOB OPPORTUNITIES
description: Available positions or possibilities for employment, which individuals can seek to increase their income.

name: GARDEN
description: A plot of ground, typically near a house, used for growing vegetables, fruits, or flowers, the produce of which can be sold for income.

name: GOVERNMENT OPTIONS
description: Various programs and services provided by government bodies that can offer financial assistance or benefits to eligible individuals.

name: JOB 1 INCOME
description: A component of an individual's total weekly income.

name: JOB 2 INCOME
description: A component of an individual's total weekly income.

name: WEEKLY EXPENSES
description: A category representing all regular outlays of money on a weekly basis, used in budgeting.

name: CELL PHONE EXPENSE
description: The cost associated with cell phone services, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: EATING OUT EXPENSE
description: Money spent on meals consumed outside the home, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: EDUCATION AND CHILDCARE EXPENSE
description: Money spent on educational services and childcare, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: ENTERTAINMENT EXPENSE
description: Money spent on leisure activities, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: GROCERIES AND SUPPLIES EXPENSE
description: Money spent on food and household necessities, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: HELPING OTHERS EXPENSE
description: Money provided to assist other individuals, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: HOUSING AND UTILITIES EXPENSE
description: Money spent on rent/mortgage and utility services like electricity, gas, or water, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: PET EXPENSE
description: Money spent on pet care and supplies, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: EMERGENCY FUND SAVINGS
description: Money set aside regularly for unexpected urgent expenses, treated as a weekly expense in the budget.

name: GOALS SAVINGS
description: Money set aside regularly for future objectives such as education or retirement, treated as a weekly expense in the budget.

name: TRANSPORT EXPENSE
description: Money spent on transportation, such as fuel, public transit, or vehicle maintenance, categorized as a weekly expense.

name: ENDING WEEKLY BALANCE
description: The remaining amount of money after subtracting all weekly expenses from total weekly income.

name: STRATEGIES FOR IMPROVING CASH FLOW
description: Methods and techniques designed to enhance the flow of money into and out of an individual's finances.

name: LARGE PERIODIC PAYMENTS
description: Significant financial outlays that occur at regular intervals, often targeted for adjustment to improve cash flow.

name: SMALLER PAYMENTS
description: Reduced financial outlays made more frequently over a period, often used as a strategy to manage large payments.

name: DUE DATES
description: The scheduled dates by which payments or obligations must be met, which can be negotiated to improve cash flow.

name: NEGOTIATE NEW DUE DATE
description: A strategy to change the payment deadline for bills with a company to align better with income receipt.

name: SPLITTING LARGE MONTHLY PAYMENT
description: A strategy to divide a significant monthly payment (e.g., rent) into two or more smaller payments due at different times.

name: CHANGE LUMP-SUM PAYMENTS
description: A strategy to convert large, one-time payments (e.g., car insurance, school tuition) into smaller, more manageable monthly installments.

name: ENERGY ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
description: A local or national program designed to help low-income families pay for part of their energy costs.

name: LEVEL PAYMENT PLANS
description: A billing method for utility bills that averages annual use over 12 months, resulting in consistent monthly payments to avoid seasonal spikes.

name: WITHHOLDING ENOUGH TAX
description: A strategy to ensure that the correct amount of income tax is deducted from paychecks to avoid underpayment or overpayment when filing taxes.

name: TAX ALLOWANCES
description: Also known as exemptions, these reduce the amount of income tax withheld from a paycheck, affecting tax obligations.

name: IRS WITHHOLDING CALCULATOR
description: An online tool provided by the IRS to help individuals determine the correct amount of income tax to withhold from their paychecks.

name: MODULE 8: EVALUATING YOUR PREPAID OR PAYROLL CARD
description: A specific section within the "Your Money, Your Goals Toolkit" focused on assessing prepaid and payroll cards.

name: PROMOTION
description: A special offer or limited-time arrangement, often involving a promotional rate, designed to attract customers to a financial product.

name: FOREIGN TRANSACTION FEES
description: Charges applied when a financial card is used for purchases or withdrawals in a country different from the issuer's or in a foreign currency.

name: COUNTRY
description: A sovereign state or nation, relevant in the context of international financial transactions and associated fees.

name: LIMITS ON USE
description: Restrictions or conditions placed on where, when, or how a financial card or service can be utilized.

name: BALANCE CHECKING SERVICES
description: The functionality provided by financial institutions that allows users to inquire about and view their current account balance through various channels like phone, online, or mobile apps.

name: DUE DATE SELECTION
description: The option for a customer to choose or modify the specific calendar date by which their recurring bill or loan payments are expected.

name: GETTING THE LOAN
description: The complete process of applying for, undergoing approval, and successfully receiving funds from a loan.

name: LOAN APPROVAL PROCESS
description: The series of steps a lender takes to evaluate a loan application and determine whether to grant the loan.

name: LOAN LENGTH
description: The specified duration or term over which a loan is structured to be repaid.

name: OWE MONEY
description: The financial state of having an outstanding debt or obligation that needs to be settled.

name: EXCHANGING CURRENCIES
description: The act of converting funds from one national currency to another, typically for international transactions, often incurring fees.

name: RECEIVING FUNDS
description: The act of a designated recipient obtaining the money that has been sent through a money transfer service.

name: FUNDS ARRIVAL SPEED
description: The time taken for money to be transferred and become available to the recipient, a key consideration for money transfer services.

name: MONTHLY FEES
description: A regular charge applied each month for maintaining a financial account or for specific services associated with it.

name: DORMANCY FEE
description: Another term for an inactivity fee, levied on accounts considered dormant due to a lack of activity.

name: SPENDING LIMITS
description: Maximum amounts that can be spent using a financial card or account within a specified timeframe, such as daily or per transaction.

name: WITHDRAWAL LIMITS
description: Maximum amounts that can be withdrawn from an account using a card or service within a specified period, typically daily.

name: TRANSACTION LIMITS
description: Maximum amounts allowed for a single financial transaction, whether for spending or withdrawal.

name: SECURITY
description: The measures and systems implemented to protect financial assets, personal data, and transactions from fraud, theft, and unauthorized access.

name: UNAUTHORIZED USE
description: The risk of a financial card or account being used by someone other than the rightful owner without their permission, potentially leading to financial loss.

name: GETTING MONEY BACK
description: The process of recovering funds that have been lost due to unauthorized transactions, theft, or other financial irregularities.

name: FRIENDLINESS
description: A quality of customer service indicating a welcoming, polite, and helpful attitude from staff at financial institutions.

name: RESPECT
description: The courteous and considerate treatment customers expect to receive from financial service providers.

name: CONVENIENCE
description: The ease and practicality of accessing and using financial services, often influenced by factors like physical location, operating hours, and digital accessibility.

name: LOCATIONS
description: Physical branches or offices of financial institutions where customers can conduct business.

name: LIVE
description: Referring to the geographical area where an individual resides, important for assessing the convenience of nearby financial institution locations.

name: SHOP
description: Referring to geographical areas where an individual performs shopping activities, important for assessing the convenience of nearby financial institution locations.

name: OPENING TIMES
description: The hours of operation for a financial institution or its branches, impacting customer access and convenience.

name: ACCESS
description: The ability to readily obtain information, assistance, or services from a financial institution.

name: INFORMATION ACCESSIBILITY
description: The ease with which financial information and disclosures can be understood and obtained by diverse users, including those needing specific languages or formats.

name: LANGUAGE SUPPORT
description: The availability of financial services, information, and customer support in multiple languages to cater to a diverse clientele.

name: CUSTOMER SUPPORT CHANNELS
description: Various platforms and methods offered by financial institutions for customers to seek help or provide feedback, such as phone, text, email, or online chat.

name: TEXT
description: The act of sending or receiving short written messages, often used as a method for customer support or account notifications by financial services.

name: ONLINE CHAT
description: Real-time text-based communication conducted via a website or application, commonly offered by financial institutions for immediate customer support.

name: DOCUMENTS
description: Official papers, records, or forms required by financial institutions for identity verification, account opening, or transaction processing.

name: BILL WITH NAME AND ADDRESS
description: A utility bill or similar statement displaying a person's name and residential address, typically used as secondary identification or proof of residency for financial purposes.

name: GATHER DOCUMENTS
description: The act of collecting all necessary papers and forms in preparation for a financial process, such as opening an account.

name: SAVE TIME
description: The benefit of reducing the duration or effort required for a task, often achieved through preparedness in financial activities.

name: CREDIT SCORING FORMULAS
description: The methodologies and calculations used by credit reporting companies to determine a consumer's credit score, often penalizing high credit utilization.

name: HIGH USAGE
description: The potential negative impact on a credit score resulting from utilizing a large percentage of available credit, which is linked to a high credit utilization rate.

name: SCORING MODEL
description: The specific algorithm or methodology employed by credit reporting companies to calculate a consumer's credit score, which assesses credit utilization rates.

name: RISK OF LOWERING YOUR CREDIT SCORES
description: The exposure to a reduction in one's credit scores due to certain financial behaviors, such as maintaining a high credit utilization rate.

name: EXPANDING ACCESS TO CREDIT
description: An initiative or strategy focused on increasing the availability of credit to a broader segment of the population, including those traditionally underserved like 'credit invisibles'.

name: CREDIT INVISIBLES
description: Individuals who lack sufficient credit history for credit reporting companies to generate a credit score for them, often facing barriers to credit, housing, and employment.

name: DENIED ACCESS TO CREDIT
description: The negative outcome where consumers are unable to secure credit products due to factors like insufficient credit history, commonly faced by 'credit invisibles'.

name: HIGHER COSTS FOR BORROWING MONEY
description: The financial disadvantage faced by consumers with limited or no credit history, resulting in increased interest rates or fees on loans due to perceived higher risk.

name: BARRIERS TO HOUSING AND EMPLOYMENT
description: Obstacles encountered by individuals with poor or limited credit history when attempting to rent housing or secure certain jobs, impacting 'credit invisibles'.

name: LIMITED CREDIT HISTORY
description: A situation where a consumer's credit file lacks enough information or activity to establish a robust credit profile, often leading to being "unscorable.

name: UNSCORABLE
description: A designation for a credit file that cannot generate a credit score due to insufficient (thin) or stale data, impacting individuals with limited credit history.

name: THIN CREDIT FILE
description: A credit file characterized by a limited number of credit accounts or a short credit history, making it difficult for credit reporting companies to generate a score.

name: STALE CREDIT FILE
description: A credit file that contains no recent credit account activity, making it less relevant for current credit scoring and potentially rendering a consumer "unscorable.

name: LOAN APPROVAL
description: The act of a lender granting a loan application, signifying agreement to provide funds under specified terms, which credit reports can be used for.

name: GOOD CREDIT
description: A positive financial status indicated by a strong credit history and high credit scores, which facilitates access to loans, housing, and other financial services.

name: BAD CREDIT
description: A negative financial status characterized by a problematic credit history and low credit scores, which creates obstacles to obtaining loans, housing, and other services.

name: CREATING A POSITIVE CREDIT HISTORY
description: The deliberate process of establishing a favorable record of borrowing and repayment, often through responsible financial behaviors.

name: BOOSTING CREDIT SCORES

name: FIX ERRORS

name: IDENTIFICATION (ID)

name: REPORTS (BANKING HISTORY)

name: ACCOUNT HISTORY

name: HOUSTON, TX

description: The process of comparing offerings (e.g., terms, fees, services) from multiple financial institutions before making a decision on where to open an account.

name: TRANSACTION (ACCOUNT)
description: Any financial activity that affects the balance of an account, such as deposits, withdrawals, transfers, or payments.

name: FTC
description: A governmental organization that advises individuals to regularly review and monitor their financial statements and account activity for unusual or suspicious activity, especially when banking online.

name: FEMA APP

description: An organization that issues weather alerts, which are integrated into and provided by the FEMA app.

name: APP STORE

name: GOOGLE PLAY
description: A digital distribution platform where users can download the FEMA app for free.

name: ATM CARD
description: A financial card for which the Electronic Fund Transfer Act limits user responsibility for unauthorized activity if loss is reported quickly.

name: MISUSE OF CHECKING ACCOUNTS
description: An unauthorized activity or fraudulent use of a checking account, highlighted as a component of identity theft.

name: THEFT OF FINANCIAL DOCUMENTS

name: LOSS OF DEBIT/CREDIT CARDS

name: GOVERNMENT-ISSUED IDENTIFICATION
description: Official documents provided by a government agency, used for identification purposes, whose loss can contribute to identity theft.

name: CARD ISSUER

name: IDENTITY MONITORING SERVICE

name: PERSONAL IDENTIFICATION NUMBER (PIN)

name: ELECTRONIC BANKING

name: BILL PAYING

name: PURCHASING
description: The act of acquiring goods or services, particularly when done electronically, requiring secure access measures like passwords and PINs.

name: INSURANCE AGENCY
description: An organization that provides insurance services and should be contacted if an EFFAK or important documents are believed to be stolen or illegally accessed.

name: DEBIT OR ATM CARD
description: A financial instrument used for electronic transactions, whose unauthorized activity liability is governed by the Electronic Fund Transfer Act.

name: OPEN ACCOUNT
description: A specific type of financial account, exemplified by account #3629 from ABC Megastore, currently placed with Reliable Collections.

name: SPECIALTY CONSUMER REPORTS
description: A category of consumer reports, distinct from standard financial reports, that often includes sensitive information like arrests and convictions, used for purposes such as employment background screenings.

name: OPEN ACCOUNTS (GENERAL)
description: A general category of active financial accounts that an individual currently possesses, whose details are included in account information reports.

name: DISPUTING THE DEBT

description: The act of negotiating with a debt collector to pay a smaller amount than originally owed to fully resolve an account.

name: PAY SMALLEST DEBT FIRST

description: A debt repayment strategy where, after making all minimum payments, extra funds are applied to the debt with the highest interest rate first. Once paid off, that extra amount is added to the minimum payment of the next highest-rate debt.

name: RENT OR MORTGAGE PAYMENTS

name: MEMBERSHIP FEES
description: Recurring charges for belonging to an organization or using a service, important to track for financial planning.

name: BANKING OR BILL PAY MOBILE APPLICATIONS
description: Software applications for smartphones or devices that allow users to manage bank accounts and pay bills electronically, facilitating financial management.

name: STATEMENTS OF BILLS
description: Records providing details of financial transactions for services or products, such as utility bills or credit card statements, important for documentation.

name: ALIMONY
description: Financial support paid to a former spouse after divorce or separation.

name: RENT OR MORTGAGE
description: Regular payments made for housing, either to a landlord (rent) or a lender (mortgage).`

name: GOAL
description: A desired outcome or objective that an individual aims to achieve, often related to personal finance.

name: TOOLS AND EQUIPMENT
description: Physical or digital implements used to facilitate tasks or achieve objectives.

name: PROFESSIONAL ASSISTANCE
description: Expert help or advice from qualified individuals in a specific field.

name: IDENTIFY AREAS TO CUT BACK
description: The process of pinpointing specific categories of spending where reductions can be made.

name: PAY BILLS ON TIME
description: The act of remitting payment for financial obligations by their specified due dates.

name: AVOID LATE FEES
description: The act of preventing the incurrence of additional charges by paying bills promptly.

description: The process of developing a detailed, step-by-step strategy to achieve a particular goal.

name: PAST DUE BALANCES

name: PROBLEM WITH A FINANCIAL PRODUCT OR SERVICE
description: An issue or negative experience encountered with a financial product or service that may require resolution or complaint submission.

name: FINANCIAL NEGOTIATIONS AND TRANSACTIONS
description: The processes of discussing, agreeing upon, and carrying out financial agreements, such as buying or selling financial products or services.

name: FINANCIAL SERVICES COMPANY
description: An organization that provides a range of financial services, including but not limited to banking, investments, and insurance.

name: FINANCIAL COUNSELOR OR ADVISER
description: A professional who provides guidance and advice on financial matters, investments, or debt management to individuals.

name: FOREIGN PRINCE
description: A hypothetical royal figure often featured in phishing or advance-fee fraud scams, promising wealth in exchange for initial payments.

name: LOTTERY
description: A game of chance in which participants buy tickets for a random drawing, often featured in scams promising winnings for a fee.

name: CREDIT OR DEBIT CARD
description: A plastic card used for electronic financial transactions, either drawing directly from a bank account (debit) or borrowing money (credit).

name: ELECTRONIC CHECK
description: A digital version of a paper check, used to make payments directly from a bank account.

name: WIRING MONEY
description: The electronic transfer of funds from one person or institution to another, often irreversible and favored by scammers.

name: PREPAID OR GIFT CARD
description: A card pre-loaded with a specific amount of money, often requested by scammers for untraceable payments.

name: ROBOCALLS OR PRE-RECORDED MESSAGES
description: Automated telephone calls or voice messages, often used in scams, which legitimate debt collectors for the IRS will not use.

name: AUTHENTICATION NUMBER
description: A unique identifier or code used to verify the legitimacy of a communication or transaction, particularly from official entities like the IRS.

name: SCAMMERS
description: Individuals who perpetrate scams, using deception and fraud to trick others into parting with money or personal information.

name: PAPERWORK OR ELECTRONIC DOCUMENTS
description: Formal written or digital records, contracts, or agreements that outline the terms and conditions of financial deals or services.

name: FAST PROFITS
description: The promise of quick and substantial financial gains, often a characteristic of investment scams.

name: LITTLE OR NO RISK
description: A claim that an investment or opportunity carries minimal or no potential for loss, often a deceptive characteristic of scams.

name: COMPLAINT AGAINST THE COMPANY
description: A formal expression of dissatisfaction or grievance filed against a business or organization, often recorded by consumer or regulatory agencies.

name: CHOOSING FINANCIAL PRODUCTS AND SERVICES MODULE
description: A module (Module 8) that provides guidance and steps for individuals to select appropriate financial products and services.

name: ATM TRANSACTION
description: A financial activity conducted at an Automated Teller Machine, such as withdrawing cash or checking a balance.

name: AUTOMATIC BILL PAYMENT
description: A regularly scheduled payment made from an account to a biller without requiring manual initiation for each payment.

name: ELECTRONIC WITHDRAWAL
description: The removal of funds from an account through electronic means.

name: IN-PERSON WITHDRAWAL
description: The removal of funds from an account by visiting a physical bank branch or interacting with a teller.

name: OVERDRAFT PROTECTION
description: A specific type of overdraft service where overdrafts in a checking account are covered by funds transferred from a linked savings account.

name: RECURRING PAYMENT
description: A payment that is set up to occur repeatedly at predefined intervals, such as monthly or annually.

name: DIRECT BILLING ARRANGEMENT
description: A contractual agreement where a service provider or biller is authorized to directly charge an account for ongoing services or goods.

name: OPEN-LOOP PREPAID CARD
description: A type of prepaid card featuring a network logo (e.g., Visa, MasterCard) that can be used at any merchant accepting that brand.

name: GIFT CARD
description: A specific type of prepaid card, often closed-loop, issued by a retailer or bank for purchases at specific stores or groups of stores.

name: CLOSED-LOOP PREPAID CARD
description: A type of prepaid card that is restricted for use only at specific locations, such as a particular store or transportation system.

name: GENERAL PURPOSE RELOADABLE CARD (GPR CARD)
description: A common type of open-loop and reloadable prepaid card, often used for everyday spending and direct deposit.

name: CON ARTISTS AND SCAMMERS
description: Individuals or groups who employ creative and innovative schemes and appeals to defraud people of their money.

name: PROFITS
description: Financial gain, often promised to be fast and high by scammers to entice investments.

name: USA.GOV/STATE-CONSUMER
description: A government website that provides links to state consumer protection offices for individuals seeking assistance or information.

name: VIRUS
description: Malicious software that scammers can use to infiltrate computers or devices to steal sensitive personal data.

name: CONSUMER.FTC.GOV/SCAM-ALERTS
description: A website maintained by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) where consumers can search for and report information about common scams.

name: ITIN
description: Individual Taxpayer Identification Number, an alternative identification number that may allow individuals without an SSN to open certain types of bank accounts.

name: NO-INTEREST ACCOUNT
description: A type of bank account that does not earn any interest on the funds held within it, sometimes offered to individuals without an SSN or ITIN.

name: PER-CHECK OR TRANSACTION FEES
description: Charges applied by a financial institution for each check written or for individual transactions made from an account.

name: LOW BALANCE ALERTS
description: A service provided by financial institutions that notifies an account holder when their account balance falls below a predefined threshold, helping to prevent overdrafts.

name: OPENING DEPOSIT
description: The initial amount of money required to be deposited into a new account to activate it or meet the minimum opening requirement.

name: MONITORING ACCOUNT
description: The regular review and oversight of an account's activities, balance, and transactions, often done online or through a mobile app, to manage finances and detect issues.

name: GATHERING DOCUMENTS
description: The process of collecting all necessary paperwork, such as identification and financial records, required for opening a bank account or other financial activities.

name: RESEARCHING ACCOUNT TERMS
description: The act of investigating and understanding the conditions, costs, and limitations associated with a financial product or service before committing to it.

name: TRACKING
description: The activity of monitoring and recording financial information, such as income, benefits, and spending, for budgeting or tax purposes.

name: MANAGE
description: The act of overseeing and controlling one's financial resources, including income and benefits, to achieve financial goals.

name: PAYMENT METHOD
description: A mechanism or system used to transfer money from one party to another, such as cash, checks, or direct deposits, each with its own benefits and risks.

name: CANCELLATION
description: The act of revoking or invalidating a financial instrument, such as a check, often done if it is lost or stolen.

name: REISSUANCE
description: The act of providing a new financial instrument, such as a check, after the original has been lost, stolen, or canceled.

name: CHECK CLEARANCE
description: The process by which a financial institution verifies the validity of a check and transfers funds from the payer's account to the payee's account, typically involving a waiting period.

name: SPLIT DEPOSIT
description: The act of dividing a single deposit, such as a direct deposit, into multiple accounts, like checking and savings, to facilitate saving.

name: ADDITIONAL CREDIT
description: New credit opportunities or loans that an individual may seek, the approval for which can be affected by their existing debt obligations and debt-to-income ratio.

name: COURT-ORDERED FIXED PAYMENTS
description: Mandatory recurring payments determined by a legal body, such as child support, which are counted as debt for the purpose of calculating an individual's total monthly debt payment.

name: GROSS MONTHLY INCOME
description: An individual's total income before taxes or insurance deductions, representing the full amount of earnings used as a baseline for financial calculations like the debt-to-income ratio.

name: HOME MORTGAGE
description: A loan specifically for purchasing real estate, where the property itself serves as collateral, and its payments are a significant component of debt for homeowners.

name: MORTGAGE LENDERS
description: Financial institutions specializing in providing loans for real estate purchases, who specifically evaluate both total debt-to-income and mortgage debt-to-income ratios.

name: MORTGAGE DEBT
description: The outstanding principal and interest owed on a home mortgage, used to calculate the mortgage debt-to-income ratio specifically for homeowners.

name: MORTGAGE DEBT-TO-INCOME RATIO
description: A financial metric specific to homeowners, measuring the portion of gross monthly income allocated solely to the principal and interest payments on a home mortgage.

name: CONDO OR HOMEOWNER ASSOCIATION FEES
description: Mandatory recurring payments made by property owners to an association for the maintenance and management of common areas and services, which may or may not be included in a monthly mortgage payment.

name: HUD HOUSING COUNSELOR
description: A certified professional providing guidance and assistance on housing-related financial matters, including mortgage debt management, often sought when debt ratios are high.

name: REPAYING DEBT
description: The act of systematically returning borrowed money to lenders according to an agreed-upon schedule or strategy, with the goal of eliminating financial obligations.

name: HIGH DEBT-TO-INCOME RATIO RISK
description: The risk that a high debt-to-income ratio may make it difficult for an individual to obtain additional credit due to creditors' concerns about their ability to handle more debt.

name: MISSED PAYMENT
description: The act of failing to make a scheduled payment on a bill or debt by its due date, which can lead to various consequences.

name: NEGOTIATE DUE DATE
description: The act of discussing and agreeing upon a new date for a bill payment with a creditor to better align with income receipt.

name: LOSS OF ASSETS
description: The risk of losing valuable possessions such as a car (repossession) or a house (foreclosure/eviction) due to unpaid debts or late payments.

name: HOUSING OR CREDIT COUNSELOR
description: A certified professional who provides guidance on managing housing-related financial issues and debts, helping individuals build payment plans.

name: DEBT COLLECTOR HARASSMENT
description: The risk of being subjected to illegal and abusive tactics by debt collectors, such as repeated calls or false statements.

name: SUBMIT COMPLAINT
description: The act of formally reporting an issue or grievance, such as unfair debt collection practices, to a regulatory body like the CFPB.

name: CHILD IDENTITY THEFT INQUIRY FORM
description: A specific form available on TransUnion's website for parents or legal guardians to inquire about a minor's credit file in cases of suspected identity theft.

name: DISPUTE ERRORS TOOL
description: A resource designed to help individuals initiate the process of challenging inaccuracies or errors found on their credit reports.

name: CREDIT SCORING COMPANIES
description: Organizations, such as FICO and VantageScore Solutions, that develop and utilize mathematical formulas to calculate various credit scores.

name: INFORMATION FURNISHER
description: An entity, such as a creditor, that supplies data to credit reporting companies. Consumers can file disputes with them regarding incorrect information.

name: CHECKING IN
description: The activity of regularly communicating with a friend or family member to maintain accountability for a goal.

name: MASSACHUSETTS (MA)
description: A U.S. state where State Farm Life Insurance Company is not licensed.

name: NEW YORK (NY)
description: A U.S. state where State Farm Life Insurance Company is not licensed, but State Farm Life and Accident Assurance Company is licensed.

name: WISCONSIN (WI)
description: A U.S. state where State Farm Life Insurance Company is not licensed, but State Farm Life and Accident Assurance Company is licensed.

name: ILLINOIS (IL)
description: The U.S. state where State Farm's main corporate entities are located, specifically in Bloomington.

name: TEXAS (TX)
description: The U.S. state where State Farm County Mutual Insurance Company of Texas and State Farm Lloyds are located, specifically in Richardson.

name: FLORIDA (FL)
description: The U.S. state where State Farm Florida Insurance Company is located, specifically in Tallahassee.

name: 2025 NATIONAL SURVEY BY STATE FARM
description: A survey conducted by State Farm in 2025 among new policyholders to ascertain average annual per household savings.

name: LIABILITIES COVERAGE
description: A component of insurance that covers damages or injuries caused to other people or their property.

name: HO-6 INSURANCE
description: A specific type of condo insurance coverage designed for condominium owners, working in conjunction with a master insurance policy.

name: CONDO ASSOCIATION MASTER INSURANCE POLICY
description: An insurance policy held by a condominium association that covers common areas and the building structure, complementing HO-6 insurance.

name: GOOD STUDENT DISCOUNT
description: A type of discount opportunity for auto insurance, offered to students who maintain good academic standing.

name: BUNDLING DISCOUNT
description: A type of discount opportunity for auto insurance, achieved by purchasing multiple insurance policies from the same provider.

name: SAFE DRIVER PROGRAM
description: A program offering discounts for auto insurance to policyholders who demonstrate safe driving habits.

name: TELEMATICS PROGRAMS
description: Programs that utilize technology to monitor driving behavior, offering auto insurance savings based on safe driving.

name: SWITCHING TO STATE FARM
description: The action of changing insurance providers to State Farm, often resulting in reported savings for new policyholders.

name: BUYING A CAR
description: A significant life event or large purchase that typically requires long-term planning and saving.

name: WEDDING
description: A significant life event that typically requires long-term planning and saving.

name: GRADUATION PARTY
description: A celebratory event marking a graduation, considered a life event that may require financial planning.

name: HAVING A CHILD
description: A significant life event that involves various expenses and requires financial planning.

name: BIRTH OR ADOPTION
description: A life event involving the arrival of a child into a family, which requires financial planning.

name: TOOLS FOR WORK
description: The act of acquiring significant tools needed for work, classified as a large purchase event.

name: BIRTHDAY OR RITE OF PASSAGE
description: A celebratory or transitional life event that may incur costs and requires planning.

name: BUYING A HOUSE
description: A major life event and large purchase that typically requires substantial financial planning and saving.

name: COLLEGE OR TRAINING
description: A significant life event involving educational pursuits that often require substantial financial investment.

name: LOSING OR GETTING A JOB
description: Significant life events related to employment status that impact financial circumstances and may necessitate goal revision.

name: EARNING MORE MONEY
description: A change in financial circumstances where income increases, potentially affecting goal attainability.

name: HEALTH EMERGENCY
description: An unforeseen medical crisis that can significantly alter financial circumstances and necessitate goal adjustments.

name: COMMISSION
description: A government body listed alongside the Office of Personnel Management, White House Office of Public Engagement, and FEMA as members of a group

name: DHS VISUAL IDENTITIES
description: Intellectual property owned by the Department of Homeland Security, comprising its official seal, insignia, and trademarks, whose unauthorized use is strictly prohibited and constitutes a violation of Federal law.

name: UNITED STATES GOVERNMENT
description: The overarching federal government body that includes DHS and FEMA, and whose endorsement is not implied by the EFFAK.

name: VIOLATION OF FEDERAL LAW
description: The prohibited act of using the Department of Homeland Security's seal or other visual identities without proper authorization, which is against Federal law (e.g., 18 U.S.C. §§ 506, 701, 1017).

name: CUSTOMER SERVICE AGENT
description: An individual employed by a financial institution or card provider to assist customers with inquiries, issues, or account management.

name: SPENDING MONEY
description: The general action of using available funds to purchase goods, services, or make payments.

name: ELECTRONIC PAYMENTS
description: Methods of transferring funds electronically, records of which are used for income tracking.

name: GOVERNMENT PROGRAM BENEFITS
description: Financial assistance or services provided by government programs, such as Disability insurance, Social Security, TANF, and SNAP, which are considered sources of income.

name: DISABILITY INSURANCE
description: A type of government program benefit, including SSI and SSDI, that provides income in the event of an individual becoming disabled.

name: BENEFITS.GOV
description: A government website recommended by the CFPB for individuals to check if they qualify for additional benefits.

name: CUT BACK
description: The act of reducing spending or expenses in identified areas to improve financial management, as suggested by the Spending Tracker tool.

name: HELP FROM FAMILY OR FRIENDS
description: Financial assistance or support received from relatives or acquaintances, serving as an income source.

name: NOTARY SERVICES
description: Additional services sometimes offered by financial institutions to authenticate documents and signatures, which may incur a cost.

name: SAFE DEPOSIT BOXES
description: Secure containers offered by financial institutions for customers to store valuables, typically for an annual fee.

name: FEE FOR GOING BELOW MINIMUM BALANCE
description: A penalty incurred by account holders when their balance drops below a specified minimum requirement.

name: FEE FOR INACTIVITY
description: A charge applied to an account that has not seen any activity for a specified period, serving as a penalty for non-use.

name: PAY BALANCE IN FULL
description: The act of remitting the entire outstanding amount owed on a credit account, usually to avoid interest charges.

name: CHECK CASHING
description: The service of converting a check into physical currency, often offered by financial service providers and potentially incurring a fee.

name: CHECK ACCOUNT BALANCES
description: The act of verifying the current amount of money in a financial account, which can be done via phone, online, or a mobile app.

name: DEPOSIT (ACTION)
description: The act of placing money into a financial account, which may incur a fee.

name: MOBILE APPS
description: Digital applications used to keep track of bills and spending, and to send money. They may charge fees and require personal information or access to financial accounts. Users should not use them on public Wi-Fi and should protect them with strong passwords.

name: PUBLIC WI-FI
description: An insecure internet connection that poses a risk for conducting financial transactions, and should be avoided for such activities.

name: TWO-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION
description: A two-step security process (password plus a code sent via text/email) offered by many financial service providers to provide an extra layer of protection for accounts.

name: REMOTE ACCESS FEATURE
description: A capability on most mobile devices that allows users to lock the device, delete sensitive information, or activate an alarm if the device is lost or stolen, requiring activation by the user.

name: FINANCIAL TRANSACTIONS
description: The act of transferring money or managing financial resources through digital tools. It should not be conducted on public Wi-Fi due to security risks.

name: SCORING COMPANIES
description: Organizations, such as Fair Isaac Corporation and VantageScore Solutions, that develop and implement mathematical formulas to calculate credit scores.

name: AMOUNTS OWED
description: A significant factor in FICO scores, encompassing an individual's total debts and their credit utilization rate.

name: LENGTH OF CREDIT HISTORY
description: A factor in FICO scores that measures the duration of an individual's credit accounts, providing evidence of long-term credit behavior.

name: NEW CREDIT
description: A factor in FICO scores that tracks recent credit inquiries and newly opened credit accounts, which can indicate a high demand for credit.

name: TYPES OF CREDIT USED
description: A factor in FICO scores that considers the variety of credit accounts an individual manages, such as revolving credit and installment loans, contributing to a "credit mix.

name: CREDIT INQUIRIES
description: Records of requests for an individual's credit report, made by creditors, which can impact "New credit" and "Recent credit behavior and inquiries" scores.

name: CREDIT MIX
description: The combination of different types of credit accounts (e.g., revolving and installment) held by an individual, which can positively influence credit scores.

name: AGE AND TYPE OF CREDIT
description: A "highly influential" factor for VantageScore, combining the duration of credit accounts and the variety of credit types.

name: PERCENT OF CREDIT LIMIT USED
description: A "highly influential" factor for VantageScore, representing the proportion of available credit that is currently being utilized.

name: TOTAL BALANCES/DEBT
description: A "moderately influential" factor for VantageScore, representing the overall amount of money an individual owes across all accounts.

name: RECENT CREDIT BEHAVIOR AND INQUIRIES
description: A "less influential" factor for VantageScore, encompassing recent credit activity and requests for credit reports.

name: MYFICO.COM
description: An online resource providing information about FICO credit scores, referenced as a source for FICO's calculation methodology.

name: CHECKING CREDIT REPORTS
description: The act of an individual reviewing their own credit reports, which does not negatively affect their credit score.

name: PAYING FOR CREDIT SCORES
description: The act of purchasing a credit score from a third-party provider, though these may differ from scores used by lenders.

name: GETTING FREE CREDIT SCORES
description: The process of obtaining a credit score without charge, often provided by credit card companies or when a loan is denied based on the score.

name: DENYING A LOAN
description: The decision by a lender to refuse a loan application, often directly influenced by the applicant's credit score.

name: BUILDING CREDIT
description: The ongoing process of establishing, improving, and maintaining a positive credit history to achieve higher credit scores.

name: FIXING ERRORS
description: The action of identifying and correcting inaccuracies or discrepancies found within a credit report.

name: APPLYING FOR A LOAN
description: The formal process initiated by an individual to request financial borrowing from a lender.

name: NON-PAYMENT
description: The failure to make required payments on credit accounts, leading to severe negative consequences for credit scores and financial standing.

name: HIGH DEMAND FOR CREDIT
description: A situation indicated by a large number of recent credit inquiries, suggesting financial strain or excessive borrowing, which can lower credit scores.

name: HIGH CREDIT USAGE
description: The practice of utilizing a significant portion of available credit, which is negatively viewed by credit scoring models and can lead to lower scores.

name: ATM WITHDRAWAL FEE (IN-NETWORK)
description: The fee charged for withdrawing money from an ATM that belongs to the card's designated network.

name: ATM WITHDRAWAL FEE (OUT-OF-NETWORK)
description: The fee charged for withdrawing money from an ATM that is outside of the card's designated network.

name: RELOAD FEE (CASH AT RETAIL)
description: The charge for adding cash to a prepaid card at a retail store location.

name: CURRENCY CONVERSION FEE
description: A fee, usually a percentage, charged for spending or withdrawing money in a foreign country.

name: LOST, STOLEN, OR DAMAGED CARD
description: The situation where a card is compromised, potentially leading to unauthorized charges and requiring replacement.

name: UNAUTHORIZED CHARGES
description: Transactions made on a card account without the cardholder's permission, which can occur if a card is lost or stolen.

name: ERROR RESOLUTION RIGHTS
description: Protections that allow cardholders to recover money lost due to errors or theft, particularly for registered prepaid cards or payroll cards.

name: MONTHLY CARD FEE
description: A recurring charge for maintaining a card account, which some prepaid cards impose and payroll cards typically do not.

name: ADDITIONAL CARD FOR AUTHORIZED USER
description: A supplementary card issued to another individual, allowing them to spend from the primary card account.

name: UNUSED BALANCE CHECK FEE
description: The charge for receiving a check for any remaining funds on a card after it has been canceled.

name: BILL PAYMENT FEE (PROVIDER WEBSITE)
description: The charge for paying bills through the card provider's official website.

name: TRANSACTION DECLINED DUE TO INSUFFICIENT FUNDS
description: The rejection of a transaction because the card account lacks sufficient money to cover the purchase or withdrawal.

name: TRACKING INCOME AND EXPENSES (PROCESS)
description: The foundational activity of systematically recording all incoming money and outgoing expenditures over a period, typically a month, to understand financial patterns and prepare for budgeting.

name: ANALYZING CASH FLOW (PROCESS)
description: The activity of reviewing a cash flow budget to identify periods of financial shortfall, understand where money is going, and pinpoint opportunities for expense reduction or adjustment.

name: TARGET LEVELS OF SPENDING
description: Predetermined amounts or limits set for spending in various expense categories, which serve as goals within a budget to control outflows.

name: SETTING TARGETS FOR SPENDING (PROCESS)
description: The activity of establishing specific financial goals or limits for how much money will be spent on different categories of expenses within a budget to guide future spending decisions.

name: LOANING MONEY
description: The act of providing funds to another party with the expectation of repayment, often with interest.

name: RENTING PROPERTY
description: The act of leasing a house or apartment from a landlord, which may require a credit report check.

name: OPTOUTPRESCREEN.COM
description: An online service allowing consumers to opt out of receiving pre-screened offers of credit and insurance.

name: LOAN OFFERS
description: Proposals from lenders to provide credit with specific terms and interest rates, influenced by an applicant's credit report.

name: OBTAINING CREDIT
description: The process of securing a loan, credit card, or other form of borrowing, which is affected by an individual's credit history.

name: 515 C ST, NE, BIG CITY, WI
description: The specific street address for the Big City Wisconsin Court, where Miguel Smith's bankruptcy was filed.

name: 4326 FOURTH STREET, SW, BIG CITY, WI
description: The specific street address for the Big City Municipal Court, where Miguel Smith's civil judgment was recorded.

name: CIVIL JUDGMENT
description: A general legal ruling that obligates one party to pay another, often appearing as negative information on a credit report.

name: START OFF SMALL STRATEGY
description: A gradual approach to financial management or debt repayment that involves initiating with small, manageable steps to build momentum and avoid feeling overwhelmed.

name: INSURANCE PAYMENTS
description: Regular, typically recurring, fees paid to an insurance provider in exchange for coverage against specified risks or losses.

name: SAVE ON UTILITY COSTS
description: The act of taking deliberate measures to reduce the expenditure on essential services like electricity, water, or gas, through conservation or program enrollment.

name: SET ASIDE MONEY
description: The act of deliberately allocating or saving a portion of one's income or other funds for a specific future purpose or to cover anticipated expenses.

name: QUARTERLY EXPENSES
description: Costs that occur and are typically paid once every three months, such as certain tax payments or insurance premiums.

name: EMERGENCY EXPENSES
description: Unforeseen and urgent costs that require immediate payment, often necessitating funds from savings or leading to debt if no savings exist.

name: MISSING OR DELAYING PAYMENTS
description: The financial hazard of failing to submit required payments by their due date, which can result in late fees, damage to credit, or service discontinuation.

name: MONTHLY MINIMUM PAYMENT
description: The smallest sum of money a borrower is required to pay on a revolving credit account (like a credit card) each month to avoid penalties and remain in good standing.

name: FINANCIAL TRADE-OFFS
description: The necessity of making a choice between two or more desirable financial options, where selecting one means foregoing the benefits of the others.

name: PLAN TO MAKE DEBT PAYMENTS
description: A structured strategy developed to manage and execute the repayment of outstanding debts, often involving prioritization and budgeting.

name: WEIGH RISK OF LATE DEBT PAYMENT
description: The cognitive process of evaluating the potential negative consequences and implications of failing to make a debt payment by its due date.

name: PIN NUMBER
description: A Personal Identification Number used with a prepaid card, essential for protecting benefits from unauthorized use if the card is lost or stolen.

name: ERROR (CARD)
description: A mistake or discrepancy on a prepaid card account, which is often protected if the card is registered and the error is reported immediately.

name: NEEDS-TESTED BENEFITS
description: Benefits provided based on financial need, with varying protection levels for prepaid card use depending on whether they are state/local or federally administered.

name: STATE OR LOCAL LAW
description: Legislation under which certain needs-tested benefit programs are established.

name: STATE OR LOCAL AGENCY
description: An entity that administers needs-tested benefit programs (like SNAP, TANF, or WIC) which may not be subject to federal prepaid card protections.

name: PAYROLL DEDUCTION
description: Money an employer subtracts from an employee's pay to cover mandatory items (like taxes) or voluntary contributions (like insurance premiums or retirement accounts).

name: FEDERAL INCOME TAX
description: A mandatory deduction from pay imposed by the federal government.

name: FICA (FEDERAL INSURANCE CONTRIBUTIONS ACT)
description: A federal act that mandates contributions for Social Security and Medicare from both employees and employers.

name: STATE INCOME TAX
description: A mandatory deduction from pay imposed by most states.

name: HEALTH, DENTAL, OR VISION INSURANCE
description: Voluntary insurance products covering health, dental, or vision care, for which premiums can be deducted from pay.

name: HEALTH SAVINGS ACCOUNT (HSA)
description: A tax-advantaged savings account, with voluntary contributions, used to pay for healthcare costs.

name: FLEXIBLE SPENDING ARRANGEMENT (FSA)
description: An employee benefit program that allows pre-tax contributions for healthcare or childcare costs.

name: EMPLOYER-SPONSORED RETIREMENT ACCOUNT
description: A retirement savings plan offered by an employer, such as a 401k or 403b plan, allowing for voluntary contributions.

name: 401K OR 403B PLANS
description: Specific types of employer-sponsored retirement accounts that allow employees to make voluntary pre-tax contributions.

name: UNPAID DEBTS
description: Financial obligations that have not been fulfilled, which can result in legal actions such as wage garnishments.

name: POLICY PROVISIONS
description: The specific terms and conditions detailed within an insurance policy, which vary by state and define coverage.

name: RENTAL EXPENSES
description: The costs incurred for renting a car when one's own vehicle is disabled, partially covered by Rental Reimbursement.

name: DAILY ALLOWANCES
description: The specific per-day amount provided by Rental Reimbursement coverage for rental expenses, which is based on the selected coverage.

name: IMPORTANT TERMS
description: Key vocabulary and concepts related to auto insurance policies, such as coverage limits and liabilities, essential for understanding policies.

name: POLICY
description: The formal contract or agreement that outlines the terms, provisions, exclusions, and conditions of an insurance coverage.

name: PERSONALIZED SERVICE
description: Tailored assistance and advice offered by local insurance agents to meet individual customer needs.

name: VEHICLE ROLLOVER
description: A specific type of incident involving a vehicle, often mentioned as a cause of damage not covered by Comprehensive coverage if it's the sole cause.

name: LOCKED CAR
description: A situation where a vehicle's occupants are unable to access it, requiring assistance that can be covered by Emergency Roadside Service.

name: FOREIGN DRIVERS
description: Individuals who have recently arrived in the United States and require specific information and guidance regarding car insurance requirements.

name: FIRST TIME CAR INSURANCE BUYERS
description: Individuals purchasing car insurance for the very first time, who can benefit from tips and educational information.

name: CUSTOMER
description: An individual who seeks, selects, and purchases insurance products and services, or interacts with State Farm for their needs.

name: ELECT NO DEDUCTIBLE
description: The choice available to an insured person to opt out of paying a deductible for Comprehensive coverage.

name: SELECT COVERAGE
description: The act of choosing specific insurance options and products, which determines the benefits and allowances like daily rental rates.

name: USE PERSONAL VEHICLE TO DRIVE FOR A RIDESHARE COMPANY
description: The specific activity of operating one's personal car for services like Lyft or Uber, which requires specialized rideshare driver coverage.

name: SAVE
description: The act of reducing costs or expenses, a goal often discussed in the context of choosing an auto insurance policy.

name: HELP DECIDE
description: The process of assisting someone in making a choice, such as selecting the appropriate auto insurance policy.

name: AVOID MISTAKES
description: To prevent errors or inaccuracies, particularly emphasized when completing an auto insurance application.

name: STRUCK AS A PEDESTRIAN
description: An incident where a person walking is hit by a vehicle, potentially resulting in injuries or damages covered by uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage.

name: MANUFACTURER
description: A company that produces goods; information from a manufacturer is not superseded by the general insurance advice.

name: SAVE FOR GOALS
description: The activity of accumulating funds to achieve specific future objectives.

name: RESPONSIBILITIES
description: Obligations that individuals have when interacting with financial products and services.

name: FINDING FINANCIAL PRODUCTS AND SERVICES
description: The process of identifying suitable financial tools and services to meet specific needs.

name: EVALUATING PREPAID OR PAYROLL CARD
description: The process of assessing the suitability and features of prepaid or payroll cards.

name: KNOWING PREPAID CARD RIGHTS
description: The act of being informed about consumer protections and entitlements specifically related to prepaid cards.

name: PAYROLL CARDS
description: Cards onto which employers deposit employee wages, serving as an alternative to traditional bank accounts for receiving pay.

name: EBT CARDS
description: Electronic Benefit Transfer cards used by recipients to access government benefits, such as food stamps.

name: MONEY TRANSFERS
description: Services that facilitate the electronic movement of funds from one person or entity to another.

name: REPAIR CREDIT HISTORY
description: The activity of taking steps to improve a damaged or poor credit record over time.

name: PROOF OF IDENTITY
description: Documents required to verify an individual's identity, such as a driver's license or passport.

name: PROOF OF ADDRESS
description: Documents required to confirm an individual's residential location, such as a utility bill or lease agreement.

name: MINIMUM DEPOSIT
description: The smallest amount of money required to open a new financial account.

name: INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE
description: The U.S. government agency responsible for collecting taxes and enforcing tax laws.

name: PROPER IDENTIFICATION
description: The necessary and acceptable forms of documentation required to verify an individual's identity for financial transactions.

name: BETTER BUSINESS
description: An organization mentioned alongside the State Attorney General's Office and CFPB as potentially taking action against other entities.

name: FCC
description: The Federal Communications Commission is an organization that can be called to check eligibility for the "Lifeline" phone rate.

name: HUD’S HOUSING COUNSELING OFFICE
description: An office under the Department of Housing and Urban Development that provides information about public housing and eligibility.

name: AMERICAN JOB CENTERS
description: Organizations that assist with job searching and provide information about requirements for different careers.

name: NATIONAL FOUNDATION FOR CREDIT COUNSELING (NFCC)
description: An organization that provides services for debt management.

name: LEGAL SERVICES CORPORATION
description: An organization that funds Legal Services programs providing legal assistance.

name: LEGAL SERVICES PROGRAM
description: A program offering legal assistance, funded by the Legal Services Corporation.

name: CHIP PROGRAMS
description: Children's Health Insurance Program, a state and federal program providing health coverage for children, with information available at medicaid.gov.

name: SHIP PROGRAMS
description: State Health Insurance Assistance Programs that provide local help with Medicare.

name: STUDENT DEBT
description: A type of financial obligation related to education, with more information available through the CFPB.

name: DEBT MANAGEMENT
description: The process of handling and reducing an individual's outstanding debts, supported by organizations like NFCC.

name: JOB INCOME
description: Money earned from employment.

name: HEALTH CARE BILLS
description: Expenses incurred for medical services and treatments.

name: VARIABLE INTEREST RATE
description: An interest rate that can increase or decrease over the life of the loan, common for private student loans.

name: GRADUATED PAYMENT PLANS
description: A repayment plan offered by some private lenders where payments start low and gradually increase over the loan term.

name: FORBEARANCE OPTIONS
description: Options offered by private lenders that allow borrowers to temporarily delay some loan payments.

name: VETERANS’ ASSISTANCE BENEFITS
description: Specific federal benefits that can be garnished by the Department of Education for unpaid federal student loans.

name: SCHOOL
description: An educational institution that can be a lender for private student loans and whose permanent closure can lead to loan forgiveness.

name: LEGAL AID ORGANIZATION
description: An organization providing legal assistance, for example, to individuals considering filing for bankruptcy regarding student loan debt.

name: TEACHER
description: A professional working full-time in certain educational settings for five complete and consecutive academic years, who may qualify for teacher loan forgiveness.

name: BANKRUPTCY LAWYER
description: A legal professional who advises individuals on the process of filing for bankruptcy, especially concerning student loan debt.

name: LOAN CANCELLATION
description: An event where a borrower is no longer expected to repay a loan due to specific circumstances, similar to loan forgiveness or discharge.

name: LOAN DISCHARGE
description: An event where a borrower is no longer expected to repay a loan due to specific circumstances, similar to loan forgiveness or cancellation.

name: TEACHER LOAN FORGIVENESS
description: A specific program allowing teachers to have their federal student loans forgiven under certain employment conditions.

name: PUBLIC SERVICE LOAN FORGIVENESS
description: A program allowing borrowers working in the public service sector (government agency or non-profit) to have Direct Loans forgiven after 120 qualifying payments.

name: TOTAL AND PERMANENT DISABILITY
description: A medical condition that, when confirmed, can lead to the forgiveness or discharge of student loans.

name: DEATH
description: The passing of a borrower, which allows for the loan to be forgiven, cancelled, or discharged on their behalf.

name: FILING FOR BANKRUPTCY
description: The legal process undertaken by an individual who cannot repay their debts; it is generally very difficult to eliminate federal student loan debt through this process.

name: MISSING PAYMENTS
description: Failure to make scheduled loan payments, which can hurt a borrower's ability to qualify for alternative repayment plans and lead to collections.

name: AUTO FULL COVERAGE
description: Auto full coverage is a common term referring to a combination of auto insurance coverages, typically including liability, collision, and comprehensive coverage, designed to protect individuals and their vehicles in various situations.

name: MED PAY
description: Med Pay (Medical Payments coverage) is an optional auto insurance add-on that covers medical expenses for you or your passengers after an accident, regardless of fault.

name: PERSONAL INJURY PROTECTION
description: Personal Injury Protection (PIP) is an optional auto insurance add-on that covers medical expenses for you and passengers, regardless of fault, often serving a similar purpose to Med Pay.

name: CAR RENTAL REIMBURSEMENT COVERAGE
description: Car rental reimbursement coverage is an optional auto insurance add-on that covers rental costs while your vehicle is being repaired after a covered claim.

name: FLOODS
description: Floods are risks of a vehicle being damaged by water, typically covered under comprehensive auto insurance.

name: FINANCE
description: To finance a car means to obtain funds, often through a loan, to purchase it, which typically results in lenders requiring collision and comprehensive insurance.

name: CLAIM
description: A claim is a formal request made by a policyholder to an insurance company for payment or services under the terms of the insurance policy, often involving a deductible.

name: TOTALED
description: Totaled is the event when a vehicle's repair costs exceed its market value, leading to it being declared a total loss by the insurer, and potentially triggering gap coverage.

name: LENGTH OF TIME
description: The duration over which an individual plans to save money to achieve a specific financial goal, which can be adjusted.

name: ADJUSTING GOALS
description: The process of modifying financial objectives and plans in response to changing personal or financial circumstances.

name: REPLENISH EMERGENCY SAVINGS
description: The act of refilling an emergency fund after it has been utilized, ensuring financial readiness for future unexpected events.

name: EXISTING SAVINGS
description: Funds that have already been accumulated towards a financial goal, which can be reallocated if that goal changes or is no longer relevant.

name: MONEY ON HAND
description: Funds that are readily available for use to cover expenses and bills.

name: PAYING BY MAIL
description: A method of bill payment involving sending a physical check or money order through postal services.

name: IN-PERSON BILL PAYMENT
description: A method of bill payment where the payer physically goes to a designated location to make a payment, often using cash.

name: PERSON
description: An individual to whom money is owed, as might be the case for some personal debts or services.

name: AMOUNT DUE
description: The precise monetary value that is required to be paid for a bill or outstanding debt.

name: PAYMENT LOCATION
description: A physical place where an individual can go to make a payment for their bills, particularly for cash payments.

name: GAS MONEY
description: Funds specifically allocated or spent on fuel for transportation, often an additional cost for in-person bill payments.

name: TRANSIT FARE
description: The cost incurred for using public transportation, an expense related to reaching a payment location.

name: ACCOUNT FUNDS
description: Money held within a bank account, such as a checking account, available for payments and withdrawals.

name: OWNER
description: The person who legally possesses a safe deposit box and whose access rights may be subject to state laws or determined by illness or death.

name: TRUSTED FRIEND OR RELATIVE
description: An individual, close to the family, who can be entrusted with paper copies of important documents from an Emergency Financial First Aid Kit for safekeeping.

name: FINANCIAL PAPERWORK
description: A general term for documents related to an individual's financial affairs, which should be reviewed regularly for accuracy and currency.

name: ACCOUNT RECORDS
description: Official documentation detailing transactions and balances for bank accounts or other financial accounts, important to keep with an Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: STATEMENTS
description: Periodic summaries, typically from banks or other financial institutions, detailing account activity, which should be regularly downloaded or printed.

name: YOUR BIRTHDAY
description: An annual personal observance suggested as a regular reminder to review and update the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: START OF A NEW YEAR
description: The beginning of a calendar year, suggested as a regular reminder to review and update the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: CHANGE IN RESIDENCE
description: A relocation to a new home or apartment, necessitating prompt updates to various documents and insurance within the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: HAVE A CHILD
description: A life event involving the birth or adoption of a child, which requires updates to the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit due to new family needs and responsibilities.

name: CHILD CHANGES SCHOOLS
description: An event related to a child's education, serving as a reminder to update relevant information in the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: PURCHASE A HOME
description: The act of acquiring a residential property, which triggers updates to financial paperwork and insurance policies within the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: RENT AN APARTMENT
description: The act of securing a residential lease for an apartment, requiring updates to documents and renters insurance within the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: OPEN OR CLOSE BANK ACCOUNTS
description: The process of initiating or terminating banking relationships, which necessitates immediate updates to the financial documentation within the Emergency Financial First Aid Kit.

name: EMERGENCY PURCHASES
description: The act of buying essential goods or services during an urgent situation, for which having cash on hand is recommended.

name: "PAY BILLS"
description: "The act of settling financial obligations.

name: "SAVE MONEY"
description: "The act of accumulating funds or reducing expenditures.

name: "EVALUATE BENEFITS AND RISKS"
description: "The process of assessing the advantages and disadvantages of different bill payment methods.

name: "BILL PAYMENT METHOD"
description: "The various strategies and instruments available for settling financial obligations.

name: "CASH"
description: "Physical currency commonly used for transactions.

name: "FEES"
description: "Monetary charges incurred for financial services or transactions.

name: "MONEY ORDER"
description: "A payment instrument purchased for a specific amount, often used for secure mail payments.

name: "PREPAID CARD"
description: "A payment card loaded with a specific amount of money in advance.

name: "RECEIPT"
description: "A document providing proof of payment.

name: "THEFT RISK"
description: "The potential for money, personal, or financial information to be stolen.

name: "CHECK"
