description: "A written instruction to a bank to pay a specified sum of money from an account.

name: "CHECKING ACCOUNT"
description: "A bank account designed for frequent deposits and withdrawals for daily transactions.

name: "MAIL PAYMENT"
description: "The process of sending payments through the postal service.

name: "PROVE PAYMENT"
description: "The act of providing evidence or documentation that a payment has been completed.

name: "PAYMENT DISPUTE"
description: "A disagreement or contention regarding a financial transaction or payment.

name: "FUNDS SECURITY"
description: "The measures and conditions that protect money held in financial accounts from loss or unauthorized access.

name: "NEGATIVE BANKING HISTORY"
description: "A record of past financial mismanagement or issues associated with a bank account.

name: "NONSUFFICIENT FUND (NSF) FEES"
description: "Charges applied when an account lacks the necessary funds to cover a payment or check.

name: "OVERDRAFT FEES"
description: "Charges incurred when an account's balance falls below zero due to a withdrawal or payment.

name: "PAYMENT LOSS IN MAIL"
description: "The danger of a physical payment instrument (like a check or money order) being lost or intercepted during postal delivery.

name: "CREDIT CARD"
description: "A payment card that allows users to borrow funds up to a pre-approved credit limit.

name: "ONLINE PAYMENT"
description: "The act of conducting financial transactions over the internet.

name: "PHONE PAYMENT"
description: "The act of making payments via a telephone call.

name: "ONE-TIME PAYMENTS"
description: "Individual, non-recurring financial transactions.

name: "RECURRING (AUTOMATIC) PAYMENTS"
description: "Scheduled payments that are automatically deducted from an account at regular intervals.

name: "LATE PAYMENT RISK"
description: "The potential for penalties, fees, or negative credit implications due to delayed bill payments.

name: "UNAUTHORIZED CHARGES"
description: "Financial transactions made or attempted without the account holder's permission.

name: "DEBT"
description: "An obligation to pay money or provide services to another party.

name: "BORROW MONEY"
description: "To obtain funds from a lender with a promise of repayment, often with interest.

name: "INTEREST"
description: "The cost charged for borrowing money, or the income earned from lending money.

name: "DEBIT CARD"
description: "A payment card that directly withdraws funds from a linked checking account.

name: "REPLACE CARD"
description: "The process of obtaining a new payment card, typically due to loss, theft, damage, or expiration.

name: "UPDATE INFORMATION"
description: "The act of revising or submitting current personal, account, or payment details to an entity.

name: "BILLER"
description: "An entity or individual that issues invoices or requests payment for goods or services.

name: "INCONVENIENCE"
description: "A state or situation causing difficulty, trouble, or discomfort, such as buying a money order or setting up online payments.

name: "ONLINE OR MOBILE BILL PAYMENT"
description: "A digital service allowing bills to be paid through web browsers or dedicated mobile applications.

name: "BANK"
description: "A financial institution offering various services such as deposits, loans, and payment processing.

name: "CREDIT UNION"
description: "A member-owned financial cooperative that provides banking and credit services.

name: "ACCOUNT PROVIDER"
description: "An institution (e.g., bank, credit union) that offers and manages financial accounts for customers.

name: "ONLINE WEBSITE"
description: "A web-based platform used by account providers or billers for managing accounts and processing payments.

name: "MOBILE APP"
description: "A software application designed for mobile devices, often used for banking, payments, or account management.

name: "WARNINGS OR ALERTS"
description: "Notifications or messages provided to users about their account status or transactions.

name: "ACCOUNT BALANCE"
description: "The total amount of money currently available in a financial account.

name: "REJECTED PAYMENT"
description: "A payment attempt that failed to be processed, often due to insufficient funds, incorrect details, or other issues.

name: "DATA COLLECTION AND SHARING RISK"
description: "The potential for personal or financial data to be gathered, stored, and disseminated without adequate consent or security protocols.

name: "MOBILE DEVICE LOSS OR THEFT"
description: "The risk of a mobile device being lost, misplaced, or stolen, potentially compromising stored financial information.

name: "FINANCIAL INFORMATION ACCESS RISK"
description: "The potential for unauthorized individuals to gain access to sensitive financial data.

name: "CARD AGREEMENT"
description: "The legal document outlining the terms, conditions, fees, and responsibilities associated with a payment card.

name: "CUTTING EXPENSES"
description: "The practice of reducing expenditure to manage finances more effectively or save money.

name: "AFFORD NECESSITIES"
description: "The ability to have sufficient funds to cover essential goods and services required for living.

name: "SAVE FOR GOALS"
description: "The act of accumulating money over time specifically to achieve future financial objectives.

name: "EXPENSES"
description: "The costs incurred by an individual or organization in daily living or business operations.

name: "FINANCIAL GOALS"
description: "Specific objectives an individual or organization aims to achieve through financial planning and management.

name: "FINANCIAL SHORTFALL"
description: "A situation where available financial resources are insufficient to meet current or impending obligations.

name: "REDUCED WORK HOURS"
description: "A decrease in the number of hours an individual is employed, often resulting in lower income.

name: "TEMPORARY UNEMPLOYMENT"
description: "A period during which an individual is out of work for a limited duration, impacting their income.

name: "DECREASE SPENDING"
description: "The deliberate act of reducing the amount of money spent on goods and services.

name: "INCOME"
description: "Money received, especially on a regular basis, from work, investments, or other sources.

name: "FINANCIAL RESOURCES"
description: "Assets or funds available for use, investment, or to meet financial obligations.

name: "TRACKING EXPENSES"
description: "The systematic process of monitoring, recording, and analyzing expenditures.

name: LETTER (DEBT DISPUTE)
description: A formal written communication, often using a specific form or template, sent by a consumer to a debt collector to challenge the validity or accuracy of a claimed debt.

name: HEARING
description: A formal legal proceeding in a court where parties involved in a debt lawsuit present their cases, often requiring legal representation.

name: DOCUMENTATION OF DEBT
description: Official papers or records that serve as proof that a consumer is legally obligated to pay a particular debt, which can be requested for verification.

name: CASH UP FRONT
description: A requirement for an immediate, initial payment before any services are rendered or agreements are finalized, which is often a characteristic avoided by unbiased financial advisors.

name: LOCAL RESOURCE ORGANIZATIONS
description: Community-based entities or services available in a specific geographic area that can offer assistance with various needs, such as utility payments, job searching, or legal aid.

name: UNBIASED INDIVIDUALS AND ORGANIZATIONS
description: A general category of advisors or groups that provide objective financial information and guidance without attempting to sell products, requiring upfront payment, or having conflicts of interest.

name: DRIVE SAFE & SAVE®
description: A specific program from State Farm that allows customers to share their driving information to potentially earn discounts on their auto insurance premiums.

name: AUTO ACCIDENT
description: A specific incident involving one or more vehicles, resulting in damages or injuries, for which insurance coverage may apply, such as uninsured/underinsured motorist coverage or med pay/personal injury protection.

name: VEHICLE IS DISABLED
description: A state where a vehicle is unable to operate due to a malfunction, breakdown, or accident, necessitating roadside assistance like emergency road service.

name: LOCKSMITH
description: A service to assist with vehicle lockouts or key issues, often included as part of emergency road service coverage.

name: JUMPSTART
description: A service to restart a vehicle with a dead battery, often included as part of emergency road service coverage.

name: TIRE CHANGES
description: A service to replace a flat or damaged tire, often included as part of emergency road service coverage.

name: RENTAL REIMBURSEMENT
description: Optional auto insurance coverage that helps pay for the cost of a rental car while the insured's vehicle is being repaired after a covered loss.

name: RIDESHARING
description: Optional auto insurance coverage specifically designed for individuals who use their personal vehicles for ride-hailing services.

name: CONTACT AN AGENT
description: The process of reaching out to a State Farm agent for personalized service, information, or to discuss coverage options and obtain a quote.

name: GET AN AUTO INSURANCE QUOTE
description: The process of requesting and receiving an estimated cost for an auto insurance policy from State Farm.

name: AUTO (PRODUCT TYPE)
description: A specific product option for auto insurance listed in State Farm's quote dropdown menu.

name: RENTERS (PRODUCT TYPE)
description: A specific product option for renters insurance listed in State Farm's quote dropdown menu.

name: DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA
description: A US federal district where State Farm offers services, and where pricing and coverage options may vary.

name: AUTO INSURANCE RATES
description: The costs associated with auto insurance policies, which are calculated based on various factors and can fluctuate.

name: RISING AUTO INSURANCE PREMIUMS
description: The financial risk associated with increasing costs of auto insurance premiums, which can impact policyholders.

name: ELIGIBILITY
description: The conditions or qualifications that must be met for a customer to be approved for certain insurance coverages or discounts, which may vary by state.

name: RECURRING BILLS
description: Regular, repeated expenses such as rent, utilities, car payments, and insurance that typically have fixed due dates.

name: CASH FLOW TOOLS
description: Methods or strategies used to manage and balance the inflow and outflow of money over a period, detailed in Module 5.

name: BILL PAYMENT METHODS
description: Various ways to pay financial obligations, including cash, checks, credit/debit cards, money orders, online/mobile payments, and prepaid cards, each with benefits and drawbacks.

name: CREDIT OR DEBIT CARDS
description: Payment cards that allow users to either borrow funds (credit) or spend money directly from their bank account (debit); credit cards may incur interest if not paid in full.

name: MONEY ORDERS
description: A payment order for a specified amount of money, useful for making payments without a checking account, but often with fees.

name: ONLINE OR MOBILE BILL PAYMENTS
description: Electronic methods of paying bills via the internet or a mobile device, offering convenience but requiring sufficient funds to avoid rejected payments.

name: ACCESS TO CREDIT
description: The ability for an individual to obtain loans or other forms of credit, influenced by their payment and credit record.

name: REJECTED PAYMENT
description: A payment that is not processed or accepted, often due to insufficient funds in the account, which can lead to overdraft fees.

name: NEGATIVE BANKING HISTORY
description: A record of problematic past interactions with banking institutions, such as frequent overdrafts, which may prevent opening a new checking account.

name: FUNDS STOLEN
description: The danger of physical money, particularly cash, being illegally taken from its owner.

name: COUCH
description: An example of an item that can be acquired through a rent-to-own agreement

name: CO-SIGNING A LOAN
description: The act of agreeing to repay a loan if the primary borrower defaults

name: LOAN DOCUMENTS
description: Official papers containing the terms and conditions of a loan, which should be read carefully

name: DATA BREACH
description: An incident where sensitive information is exposed to unauthorized parties, which may lead to identity theft and warrant an initial fraud alert.

name: OFFERS OF CREDIT OR INSURANCE
description: Pre-screened solicitations for credit cards, loans, or insurance policies that credit reporting companies are required to remove consumers from when an extended fraud alert is in place.

name: NO CONSISTENCY
description: A red flag where different staff or salespeople provide conflicting information regarding pricing or other essential details of a financial product or service.

name: WON'T PUT IT IN WRITING
description: A red flag where clear information about financial products or services, especially pricing or terms, is not provided in writing despite requests.

name: PUSHED TO PURCHASE IMMEDIATELY
description: A red flag where a consumer is pressured to make an immediate, often big-ticket, purchase with urgency, implying the offer is fleeting.

name: STEERING AND COERCING
description: A red flag involving aggressive sales tactics used to manipulate a consumer into a high-cost loan, even if they qualify for a regular prime loan.

name: WARRANTIES
description: Guarantees on products that promise repair or replacement, which can be unnecessary add-ons pushed by lenders.

name: PRICING
description: The specific costs and rates associated with financial products and services, which should be consistent and transparent.

name: INSURANCE AGENCIES
description: Organizations that provide insurance services and have established rules and procedures for handling situations involving stolen documents or illegally accessed accounts.

name: STOLEN OR ILLEGALLY ACCESSED DOCUMENTS
description: The risk that important documents or an EFFAK might be taken without authorization or accessed unlawfully, necessitating immediate contact with financial institutions and insurance agencies.

name: MONITORING ACCOUNTS
description: The activity of regularly reviewing and checking financial account activity for unusual or suspicious patterns, recommended by the FTC for individuals and performed by financial institutions.

name: CLOSING ACCOUNTS
description: The action taken by financial institutions to deactivate or terminate a customer's account, often as a security measure following unauthorized activity or theft.

name: REOPENING ACCOUNTS
description: The action taken by financial institutions to reactivate a customer's account, typically after security issues have been addressed and resolved.

name: UNUSUAL OR SUSPICIOUS ACTIVITY
description: Patterns or occurrences in financial account activity that are not typical for the account holder and may indicate fraud, theft, or other security breaches.

name: LOCAL EMERGENCY MANAGER
description: An official or individual responsible for coordinating and managing preparedness, response, and recovery efforts for emergencies within a specific geographic area or community.

name: PROTECT AND RECOVER FROM EMERGENCY OR DISASTER
description: The dual actions of safeguarding oneself and assets from potential harm during an emergency or disaster, and subsequently taking steps to restore conditions to normalcy or an improved state.

name: FAMILY EMERGENCY PLAN
description: A structured document or set of guidelines developed by a household to ensure safety and coordinated action before, during, and after an emergency.

name: EMERGENCY SUPPLY KIT
description: A pre-assembled collection of essential items, such as food, water, and medical supplies, recommended to be sufficient for at least 72 hours of self-sufficiency during an emergency.

name: GET INVOLVED IN COMMUNITY'S PREPAREDNESS ACTIVITIES
description: The act of participating in local initiatives, exercises, and drills designed to enhance the collective readiness and resilience of a community against emergencies.

name: TRAINED VOLUNTEERS
description: Individuals who have received specific training to assist in emergency response, relief efforts, and community rebuilding following a disaster.

name: EMERGENCY PLANS
description: General term for structured documents or strategies developed by organizations like schools or workplaces to guide actions and ensure safety during an emergency.

name: SCHOOLS
description: Educational institutions that need to establish and implement emergency plans to protect students and staff during various incidents.

name: WORKPLACE
description: A location where individuals are employed, which should have established emergency plans to ensure the safety and continuity of operations for its employees.

name: NEIGHBORHOOD
description: A specific geographical area within a larger community, which should be considered when assessing disaster risks and developing local emergency plans.

name: COMMUNITY PREPAREDNESS EXERCISES AND DRILLS
description: Organized simulation activities conducted within a community to practice emergency response procedures and test the effectiveness of preparedness plans.

name: BUSINESS OWNERS
description: Individuals who own and operate commercial enterprises, for whom the U.S. Small Business Administration provides resources on emergency preparedness and disaster recovery.

name: FINANCIALLY PREPARE FOR EMERGENCY OR DISASTER
description: The proactive step taken by individuals or businesses to secure financial stability and resources to mitigate the impact of, and facilitate recovery from, emergencies and disasters.

name: PLANNING FOR EMERGENCIES
description: The comprehensive process of strategizing and developing structured approaches to anticipate, respond to, and recover from various types of emergencies and disasters.

name: BUSINESS LEADERS
description: Individuals in positions of authority within businesses who are responsible for implementing preparedness strategies and guiding their organizations through emergency planning.

name: TAKE ACTION (PREPAREDNESS)
description: A general step in emergency preparedness that refers to implementing the strategies and measures outlined in a developed plan.

name: BE RECOGNIZED AND INSPIRE OTHERS
description: A step in the Ready Business program encouraging businesses to serve as exemplary models of preparedness and motivate other organizations to adopt similar practices.

name: CLEANUP (AFTER DISASTER)
description: The immediate and ongoing process of removing debris, clearing affected areas, and initiating restoration efforts following a disaster event.

name: RECOVERY (AFTER DISASTER)
description: The long-term process following a disaster that involves restoring physical, social, economic, and environmental aspects of a community or business to an acceptable or improved condition.

name: STATEMENTS AND ACCOUNT ACTIVITY
description: The records of financial transactions, balances, and other movements within an account, which should be regularly reviewed for unusual or suspicious patterns to ensure security.

name: ATM ACCESS LIMITS
description: Restrictions imposed by certain cards on the number of times an ATM can be accessed within a 24-hour period.

name: FEDERAL CONSUMER PROTECTIONS
description: Safeguards provided by the federal government to protect consumers from financial risks like unauthorized transfers or errors.

name: PAPER-BASED BENEFITS
description: Traditional forms of government assistance, such as paper coupons or vouchers, replaced by EBT cards.

name: CASH BENEFITS
description: A form of financial assistance provided as cash, which can be sent to a bank account or prepaid card.

name: OWN PREPAID CARD
description: A personal prepaid card owned by an individual, which can be used to receive cash benefits to prevent them from expiring.

name: ONE-TIME ACTIVITY
description: An activity performed once, such as selling items, to generate income.

name: INCREASE YOUR REFUND
description: A financial goal or outcome achieved by claiming additional tax credits.

name: USE YOUR SKILLS AND RESOURCES
description: A strategy for increasing income by leveraging personal abilities and available assets.

name: FRIENDS OR RELATIVES
description: Individuals with whom one can exchange services or rent out a room.

name: BORROWERS
description: Individuals who have taken out federal student loans and are responsible for their repayment, potentially qualifying for various repayment plans and temporary options.

name: REVISED PAY AS YOU EARN (REPAYE)
description: An income-driven repayment plan for federal Direct Loans where payments are set at 10 percent of discretionary income, recalculated annually. Payments can be as low as $0. The loan period is up to 20 or 25 years, after which any remaining amount is forgiven, though it may be subject to income tax.

name: PAY AS YOU EARN (PAYE)
description: An income-driven repayment plan for federal Direct Loans where payments are set at up to 10 percent of discretionary income, recalculated annually. Payments can be as low as $0 and will never be higher than the Standard repayment plan. The loan period is up to 20 years, after which any remaining amount is forgiven, though it may be subject to income tax. Requires partial financial hardship and specific borrower dates.

name: INCOME-BASED REPAYMENT (IBR)
description: An income-driven repayment plan for federal student loans (excluding Parent Plus loans) where payments are based on up to 10% or 15% of discretionary income, recalculated annually. Loan periods are 20 or 25 years, after which remaining amounts are forgiven (potentially taxable). Requires partial financial hardship.

name: PARENT PLUS LOANS
description: A type of federal student loan that is explicitly not eligible for the Income-Based Repayment (IBR) plan.

name: FORGIVENESS
description: The cancellation of the remaining balance of a student loan after a specified period of repayment under certain plans, though the forgiven amount may be subject to income tax.

name: INCOME TAX LIABILITY
description: The obligation to pay taxes, specifically noted for potentially applying to amounts of student loans that are forgiven under income-driven repayment plans.

name: PARTIAL FINANCIAL HARDSHIP
description: A condition a borrower must demonstrate to qualify for specific income-driven repayment plans, indicating that their income is insufficient to make payments under other standard plans.

name: ACCRUING INTEREST
description: Interest that accumulates on a loan balance, particularly during periods when payments are temporarily stopped or reduced, such as deferment for unsubsidized loans or forbearance.

name: OCTOBER 1, 2007
description: A specific date that serves as an eligibility cutoff for borrowers seeking to qualify as a "new borrower" for the Pay As You Earn (PAYE) repayment plan.

name: OCTOBER 1, 2011
description: A specific date that serves as an eligibility cutoff for borrowers seeking to qualify for the Pay As You Earn (PAYE) repayment plan, requiring a Direct Loan payout on or after this date.

name: JULY 1, 2014
description: A specific date that differentiates the payment percentage and loan period terms for the Income-Based Repayment (IBR) plan, depending on when a borrower's Direct Loan was issued.

name: RISK OF HIGHER TOTAL PAYMENTS
description: The potential outcome in some repayment plans (e.g., Graduated, PAYE, IBR) where the total amount paid over the life of the loan exceeds that of the Standard 10-year repayment plan due to longer repayment periods or different payment structures.

name: RISK OF INCOME TAX ON FORGIVEN AMOUNT
description: The financial risk that borrowers may incur income tax on the amount of their student loan that is forgiven under specific repayment plans.

name: INTEREST ACCUMULATION RISK
description: The risk that interest will build up on a loan, particularly on unsubsidized loans during deferment or on any loan during forbearance, increasing the total amount owed.

name: LOAN PERIOD
description: The total duration over which a loan is scheduled to be repaid, varying across different repayment plans.

name: PARKING TICKETS
description: Fines issued for violating parking regulations, which should be paid on time to avoid additional charges.

name: OTHER FINES
description: Penalties for breaking rules or laws, similar to parking tickets, that incur additional charges if not paid promptly.

name: ADDITIONAL CHARGES
description: Extra costs or penalties that may be incurred if initial fees or fines are not paid on time.

name: COST PER SERVING
description: A metric used when buying groceries to compare the price of an item relative to its portion size, helping to identify true savings.

name: SALES
description: Promotional events where goods or services are offered at reduced prices, presenting an opportunity to save money on needed items.

name: WEATHERIZATION INCENTIVES OR PROGRAMS
description: Financial benefits or government-supported initiatives aimed at encouraging energy efficiency improvements in homes, which can help cut utility costs.

name: CHECK-CASHING SERVICES
description: Businesses that provide the service of cashing checks for a fee, identified as a source of avoidable fees.

name: NO-FEE BANK ACCOUNT
description: A type of bank account that does not charge regular service fees, offering a way to avoid banking costs.

name: MAGAZINE SUBSCRIPTIONS
description: Recurring payments for regular delivery of magazines, identified as a potential cost to cut if not actively used.

name: MOVIE-STREAMING SERVICES
description: Subscription-based services providing access to a library of movies and TV shows, identified as a potential cost to cut if not actively used.

name: HABITS
description: Established patterns of behavior, especially those related to spending (e.g., cooking at home vs. eating out), that can be changed to save money.

name: SKILLS
description: Abilities or proficiencies, such as cooking, crafts, or language, that individuals possess and can leverage to earn extra money.

name: TRADEOFFS
description: Situations in financial decision-making where choosing one option necessitates giving up another, requiring careful assessment.

name: ADD-ON PRODUCTS
description: A general category of unnecessary items, such as additional insurance, unneeded warranties, and monitoring services, that lenders may insist on or imply borrowers must buy, which get incorporated into the loan amount and accrue interest.

name: LIFE OF THE LOAN
description: The full duration or period over which a loan is scheduled to be repaid by the borrower.

name: COMPLAINTS (FINANCIAL)
description: Formal expressions of dissatisfaction or grievance submitted by consumers regarding issues with financial products or services.

name: MARKETPLACE (FINANCIAL)
description: The economic environment or system where financial products and services are offered, exchanged, and consumed, and where problems experienced by people are identified.

name: CFPB COMPLAINT PORTAL
description: The online platform provided by the CFPB at consumerfinance.gov/complaint, where consumers can submit and track the status of their complaints.

name: COMPANY RESPONSE
description: The formal communication from a financial company detailing the steps taken or that will be taken to address an issue identified in a consumer's complaint.

name: COMPLAINT PUBLICATION
description: The act by the CFPB of making anonymized information about consumer complaints, such as the subject and date, publicly available on its Consumer Complaint Database.

name: CONSUMER REVIEW (OF RESPONSE)
description: The process where a consumer evaluates a financial company's response to their complaint and provides feedback to the CFPB within 60 days.

name: FEEDBACK (CONSUMER)
description: Input provided by consumers to the CFPB regarding the complaint process or the company's response to their issue.

name: HOW TO SUBMIT A COMPLAINT
description: The outlined instructions and available methods (online, phone, mail) for consumers to initiate and file a complaint with the CFPB.

name: TOOLS (CFPB TOOLKIT)
description: Specific components, resources, or instruments included within the "Your Money, Your Goals: A financial empowerment toolkit" prepared by the CFPB.

name: EDUCATIONAL MATERIALS (CFPB)
description: The various resources, content, and materials prepared by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for educational and informational purposes for the public.

name: THIRD-PARTY SITES
description: Websites operated by entities other than the CFPB, which may be linked or referenced in CFPB materials but are not controlled or guaranteed for accuracy by the CFPB.

name: THIRD-PARTY ENTITIES
description: General term for external individuals or organizations whose resources or content may be linked or referenced by the CFPB, but are not endorsed or vetted by the CFPB.

name: GUIDANCE OR ADVICE (FINANCIAL)
description: Professional recommendations or counsel related to financial matters, typically provided by qualified professionals such as accountants or certified financial advisors.

name: ORGANIZATION'S POLICIES
description: Rules or guidelines that govern how an organization operates, specifically mentioned in the context of handling personal information.

name: CIRCUMSTANCES
description: The current state of an individual's financial and personal situation that can impact their ability to achieve financial goals.

name: LUMP SUM
description: A single, large payment of money, often from sources like a tax refund or inheritance, which can be strategically applied to financial goals.

name: SAVINGS GOALS
description: Specific financial objectives focused on accumulating money over time, such as saving for a car or a TV.

name: WEEKLY SAVINGS GOAL
description: A specific amount of money an individual aims to save each week towards a larger financial objective.

name: NEW $600 TV
description: A specific consumer good, in this context, representing a tangible item an individual is saving money to purchase.

name: FEES/CHARGES
description: Additional costs or amounts added to a debt, which may include administrative fees or late payment charges.

name: DEBT COLLECTION LICENSE
description: An official authorization issued by a state agency, required for a firm to legally engage in debt collection activities within that state.

name: RESOLVE ACCOUNT
description: The action of settling a debt account, potentially by paying a negotiated amount to fully satisfy the outstanding balance.

name: WRITTEN AGREEMENT
description: A formal document outlining the terms and conditions that created the original obligation to pay a debt.

name: TOTAL UP INCOME
description: The act of summing all income received over a specific period.

name: PREDICTABLE PAYMENT
description: A payment that arrives at a consistent time and in a consistent amount, making it reliable for financial planning.

name: RELIABLE INCOME
description: Income that can be consistently counted on each month due to its predictable nature.

name: ONLINE SPENDING RECORDS
description: Digital documentation of past expenditures, accessible through online platforms, used for tracking finances.

name: SPENDING TRACKING APP
description: A software application designed to help users record, categorize, and monitor their financial outgoings.

name: SHELTER
description: An essential need representing housing or living accommodations as an expense category.

name: SORT SPENDING BY CATEGORY
description: The activity of organizing expenditures into predefined groups for analysis.

name: SHARED BILLS
description: Financial obligations that are split or divided among multiple individuals.

name: TOTAL UP CATEGORIES
description: The act of summing the expenditures within each spending category.

name: MONTHLY BILLS
description: Financial obligations that are due and paid on a recurring monthly basis.

name: WEEKS WITH MOST MONEY DUE
description: Periods within a month when the cumulative total of financial obligations is highest, requiring careful cash flow management.

name: PLAN TO PAY BILLS
description: The strategic process of organizing finances to ensure timely payment of all outstanding bills.

name: BILL REMINDER
description: A feature or system that prompts an individual about upcoming bill due dates, often part of a bill calendar.

name: ONLINE BILL STATEMENTS
description: Digital versions of financial statements for bills, accessible through online accounts.

name: CABLE
description: A financial concept representing expenses for cable television or internet services, listed as an example of a bill.

name: WEEKLY BILL TOTALS
description: The sum of all financial obligations due within a specific week.

name: BIRTHDAY EXPENSES
description: Costs associated with celebrating birthdays, such as gifts or parties.

name: HOLIDAY EXPENSES
description: Costs associated with seasonal holidays, such as decorations, travel, or gifts.

name: SCHOOL EXPENSES
description: Costs associated with education, such as tuition, books, or supplies for school.

name: INFREQUENT BILLS
description: Financial obligations that are due less often than monthly, such as quarterly or annually.

name: DAILY REMINDER
description: A feature or system that provides daily prompts or notifications, often for bill payments or tasks.

name: LABEL CALENDAR WITH DATES
description: The act of marking or writing specific dates onto a calendar for organizational purposes.

name: LIST BILLS
description: The act of compiling a comprehensive record of all financial obligations.

name: MARK PAYMENT DATE
description: The act of noting or highlighting the intended payment date for a bill on a calendar or schedule.

name: ENTER INCOME INTO CALENDAR
description: The act of recording income receipt dates onto a calendar to facilitate financial planning.

name: IDENTIFY VALUES
description: The process of recognizing and defining personal priorities and what truly matters, guiding goal setting.

name: WORK TOWARDS FUTURE
description: The action of making efforts and progress towards desired long-term outcomes or goals.

name: TRACK PROGRESS
description: The act of monitoring and evaluating advancement towards a goal over time.

name: PICK STATEMENT
description: The action of selecting a specific written declaration or option as a starting point for goal setting.

name: WRITE DOWN GOAL
description: The action of articulating and recording a personal objective in written form.

name: SHARE GOAL
description: The act of communicating a personal objective to another person for support or accountability.

name: REALISTIC TIMELINE
description: A practical and achievable schedule for reaching a goal, considering all necessary steps and resources.

name: STICK TO ACTION PLAN
description: The commitment to diligently follow a predefined strategy to achieve a goal.

name: EVALUATING CARDS
description: The process of assessing the fees and protections of a prepaid or payroll card.

name: WRITING RESPONSE
description: The act of recording answers to questions about card fees and protections.

name: MANAGING CARD COST
description: The act of controlling or reducing the expenses associated with having and using a card.

name: REGISTERING CARD
description: The process of officially associating a card with the cardholder's identity to gain full protections.

name: WITHDRAWING MONEY FROM ATM
description: The act of taking cash out of an account using an Automated Teller Machine.

name: IN-NETWORK ATM
description: An Automated Teller Machine that belongs to a card's designated network, typically offering lower or no withdrawal fees.

name: OUT-OF-NETWORK ATM
description: An Automated Teller Machine that does not belong to a card's designated network, often incurring higher withdrawal fees.

name: FREE ATM WITHDRAWALS
description: A benefit allowing a certain number of cash withdrawals from an ATM without incurring a fee.

name: RELOADING CARD WITH CASH
description: The act of adding physical currency to a prepaid card balance.

name: RETAIL STORES
description: Commercial establishments that sell goods or services directly to consumers.

name: SPENDING MONEY IN ANOTHER COUNTRY
description: The act of making purchases with a card while physically located in a foreign country.

name: WITHDRAWING MONEY IN ANOTHER COUNTRY
description: The act of taking cash out of an ATM with a card while physically located in a foreign country.

name: PURCHASE TRANSACTION
description: A financial activity involving the exchange of money for goods or services.

name: WITHDRAWAL TRANSACTION
description: A financial activity involving the removal of funds from an account, typically as cash.

name: FLAT FEE
description: A fixed charge that does not vary based on the value of the transaction or service.

name: ONLINE BALANCE CHECK
description: The act of checking a card's balance through an internet-based platform or website.

name: EMAIL BALANCE REQUEST
description: The act of requesting a card's balance information via email.

name: TEXT BALANCE REQUEST
description: The act of requesting a card's balance information via text message.

name: PAPER STATEMENT
description: A physical document detailing a card's transactions and account activity, sent via postal mail.

name: ONLINE STATEMENT
description: A digital document accessible via the internet, detailing a card's transactions and account activity.

name: RECENT CARD PURCHASES LIST
description: A record of recent transactions made with a card.

name: REPLACING CARD
description: The act of obtaining a new card to substitute for a lost, stolen, or damaged one.

name: CUSTOMER SERVICE PHONE NUMBER
description: A telephone number provided for cardholders to contact customer service.

name: REPORTING LOST OR STOLEN CARD
description: The act of notifying the card provider that a card has been lost or stolen.

name: RESPONSIBILITY FOR CHARGES
description: The legal or contractual obligation to pay for transactions made on a card account.

name: RECOVERING MONEY
description: The act of getting back funds that were lost due to error, theft, or unauthorized charges.

name: CARD ACCOUNT
description: The underlying financial account linked to a prepaid or payroll card.

name: REGISTERED CARD
description: A card that has been activated and linked to the cardholder's personal information, often granting additional protections.

name: PIN
description: A Personal Identification Number used for authenticating card transactions, especially ATM withdrawals.

name: CARD ERROR
description: An inaccuracy or mistake in card transactions or account processing.

name: REPORTING PROMPTLY
description: The act of notifying the card provider quickly after discovering an issue like loss, theft, or error.

name: MANAGING CARD
description: The general oversight and administration of a card, including its usage, fees, and security.

name: WAIVING FEE
description: The act of excusing a cardholder from paying a particular charge.

name: AVOIDING INACTIVITY FEES
description: The act of performing transactions or activities to prevent inactivity charges from being applied to a card.

name: ADD AUTHORIZED USER
description: The act of granting another person permission to use a card linked to one's account.

name: VERIFYING IDENTITY
description: The process of confirming a person's identity, often required for new authorized users or card registration.

name: CANCELING CARD
description: The act of closing a card account and terminating the card's validity.

name: RECEIVING CHECK FOR UNUSED BALANCE
description: The act of getting a physical check for any remaining funds on a card after it has been canceled.

name: SPENDING REMAINING FUNDS
description: The act of using the leftover money on a card before closing the account.

name: WITHDRAWING REMAINING FUNDS
description: The act of taking out any leftover cash balance from a card before closing the account.

name: PAYING BILLS THROUGH PROVIDER WEBSITE
description: The act of making payments for bills using the online platform provided by the card issuer.

name: FREE BILL PAYMENT OPTIONS
description: Methods of paying bills that do not incur any charges.

name: BILLER DEBITING CARD ACCOUNT
description: The act of a bill-issuing entity directly withdrawing funds from a card account to cover a bill.

name: TRANSFERRING MONEY BETWEEN CARDS
description: The act of moving funds electronically from one card to another.

name: INSUFFICIENT FUNDS
description: A situation where an account lacks enough money to cover a transaction.

name: DECLINED TRANSACTION FEE
description: A charge incurred when a financial transaction is rejected, often due to insufficient funds.

name: FIXED DEPOSIT
description: A financial product where a fixed amount of money is deposited for a specific length of time, earning interest. Early withdrawal incurs a penalty.

name: EARLY WITHDRAWAL PENALTY
description: A fee charged for withdrawing money from a fixed deposit before the agreed-upon term, which can sometimes exceed the interest earned, leading to a loss of money.

name: WITHDRAW
description: The act of taking money out of an account or financial product, such as from a fixed deposit or checking account.

name: GROCERY STORE
description: A retail business that sells food and household items, some of which may offer financial services like check cashing or money transfer services.

name: RETAIL STORE
description: A business that sells goods directly to consumers, some of which may offer check cashing services, store credit cards, or money transfer services.

name: BOUNCED CHECKS
description: Checks that cannot be paid because there is insufficient money in the account from which they are drawn, typically incurring fees for the account holder.

name: NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATION
description: An organization that uses its surplus revenues to achieve its mission, potentially offering financial products like credit builder loans.

name: LATE PAYMENT FEE
description: A charge imposed for failing to make a payment by the due date on a credit card or loan account.

name: GAS STATION CHAIN
description: A network of fueling stations, some of which may issue their own branded credit cards.

name: STORE CREDIT CARD
description: A credit card issued by a retail store, often with specific benefits or discounts for purchases made at that store.

name: PAYMENT GUARANTEE
description: An assurance that a payment will be made, as offered by a money order which is pre-funded and cannot bounce like a check.

name: U.S. POSTAL SERVICE
description: An independent agency of the United States federal government responsible for postal service, which also offers financial products like money orders.

name: MONEY TRANSFER SERVICE
description: A service allowing individuals to send money to another person or location, often for a fee, with various delivery and payment options including bank accounts, mobile phones, or cash pick-up.

name: WIRE TRANSFER SERVICE
description: A specific type of electronic money transfer service that sends funds between banks or financial institutions, allowing money to be sent to another person or place.

name: HOUSE
description: A residential building, typically purchased with a mortgage, which also serves as collateral for the loan.

name: LOAN DEFAULT
description: The failure to meet the legal obligations of a loan, potentially leading to consequences like foreclosure for a mortgage or loss of collateral for a pawn loan.

name: SAVING AT TAX TIME HANDOUT
description: A supplementary document or tool designed to help individuals prioritize saving strategies when they receive a tax refund

name: FIND A PLACE FOR SAVINGS
description: A tool designed to assist individuals in selecting the most suitable place or method for keeping their accumulated savings

name: MANAGE MEDICAL BILLS
description: The act of organizing and handling payments for healthcare expenses.

name: DEAL WITH DEBT
description: The general act of addressing and resolving financial obligations, encompassing various strategies like payment plans or counseling.

name: GET A RESPONSE
description: The act of seeking and obtaining communication or clarification from financial institutions or debt collectors.

name: FIND A LAWYER
description: The act of seeking out and securing legal representation or advice.

name: CONTACT STATE ATTORNEY GENERAL'S OFFICE
description: The act of reaching out to the chief legal office of a state government for assistance with consumer issues or complaints.

name: ACCOUNT DELINQUENT
description: The status of a financial account when required payments have not been made by the due date, indicating overdue debt.

name: DOCUMENTATION PROVING DEBT
description: Official papers or records that establish the validity of a debt and the requirement for its payment.

name: RECORDS (DEBT)
description: Written or digital accounts of communications, transactions, and documents related to a debt, which are helpful for disputes or court proceedings.

name: UNSECURED DEBTS
description: Financial obligations that are not backed by collateral, such as credit card debt or certain personal loans.

name: GOVERNMENT PROGRAM TO BAIL OUT PERSONAL CREDIT CARD DEBT
description: A governmental initiative or scheme proposed to assist individuals in managing or eliminating personal credit card debt, often falsely claimed by unscrupulous companies.

name: PERSONAL CREDIT CARD DEBT
description: Revolving debt accumulated from personal spending on credit cards, not secured by any collateral.

name: RENT PAYMENTS
description: Periodic payments made for the use of property or housing.

name: FREE LEGAL SERVICES
description: Legal assistance provided without charge, often available to eligible individuals based on income qualifications.

name: PAYING FOR COLLEGE
description: The financial act of covering educational expenses for higher education, often involving student loans or other funding methods.

name: FORECLOSURE DANGER
description: The imminent threat of losing ownership of a mortgaged property due to failure to make required payments.

name: DEBT SETTLEMENT COMPANY RISKS
description: Potential negative consequences, such as further financial harm or increased debt, associated with engaging debt settlement companies that might mislead consumers.

name: DEBT COLLECTION CALLS AND LAWSUITS
description: Aggressive or persistent contact from debt collectors and the potential for legal action, posing a burden and risk to debtors.

name: FINANCIAL FUTURE
description: The prospective economic well-being and stability of individuals or families.

name: UNPREDICTABLE
description: Events or outcomes that cannot be foreseen or known in advance, which life insurance aims to protect against.

name: DEATH BENEFIT
description: The amount of money paid out by a life insurance policy to the beneficiary when the policyholder passes away.

name: PURCHASER
description: An individual who buys a life insurance policy.

name: POLICYHOLDER
description: The individual who owns a life insurance policy and whose life is insured.

name: LIFE CLAIM
description: A request made to an insurance company for payment under the terms of a life insurance policy following the insured's death.

name: FINANCIAL OBLIGATIONS
description: Debts or expenses that must be paid, which a death benefit can help cover.

name: HOUSING COSTS
description: Expenses related to living accommodations, such as rent or mortgage payments.

name: MORTGAGE PAYMENTS
description: Periodic payments made on a mortgage loan.

name: LOSS OF FINANCIAL CONTRIBUTION
description: The absence of income or financial support from an insured person due to their death.

name: LOST INCOME
description: Earnings that are no longer received due to an insured person's death.

name: LIVING COSTS
description: Regular expenses required for daily life, such as groceries and utilities.

name: SMALL BUSINESS OWNERS
description: Individuals who own and operate small businesses, for whom life insurance offers specific benefits.

name: CASH VALUE
description: A component of some life insurance policies that accumulates over time and can be accessed by the policyholder.

name: DOWNTURN
description: A decline in economic activity or business conditions.

name: NATURAL CAUSES
description: Death resulting from internal factors or disease, not external injury.

name: HOMICIDE
description: The unlawful killing of one person by another, covered by life insurance policies with certain exclusions.

name: SUICIDE
description: The act of intentionally causing one's own death, which may have an exclusion period in life insurance policies.

name: LIFE INSURANCE CLAIM DENIAL
description: The act by an insurance company of refusing to pay a life insurance claim.

name: HIGH-RISK ACTIVITIES
description: Actions or hobbies that significantly increase the likelihood of injury or death, potentially impacting life insurance coverage.

name: SKYDIVING
description: A high-risk activity involving jumping from an aircraft and descending with a parachute.

name: HEALTH CONDITION
description: The state of one's physical or mental health, relevant for life insurance applications.

name: APPLYING FOR LIFE INSURANCE
description: The process of submitting an application to an insurance company to obtain a life insurance policy.

name: SUICIDE EXCLUSION PERIOD
description: A specific timeframe, typically the first two years of a policy, during which a life insurance policy will not pay out if death is by suicide.

name: TERM LIFE INSURANCE POLICY
description: A type of life insurance that provides coverage for a specific period (term) and pays a death benefit if the insured dies during that term.

name: LIFE INSURANCE COVERAGE
description: The amount of financial protection provided by a life insurance policy.

name: COST OF HAVING LIFE INSURANCE
description: The monetary expense associated with purchasing and maintaining a life insurance policy.

name: COST OF NOT HAVING LIFE INSURANCE
description: The financial impact and potential losses incurred by a family or loved ones due to the absence of life insurance.

name: POTENTIAL POLICYHOLDER
description: An individual who is considering purchasing a life insurance policy.

name: HOME MAINTENANCE
description: The upkeep and repair of a home, a type of living cost.

name: LIFE INSURANCE POLICY EXPIRATION
description: The termination of a life insurance policy at the end of its term or due to non-payment.

name: FORGOING LIFE INSURANCE
description: The act of choosing not to obtain a life insurance policy.

name: AGE TO GET LIFE INSURANCE
description: The optimal or recommended age at which an individual should purchase life insurance.

name: LIFE INSURANCE NEEDS CALCULATOR
description: A tool used to estimate the appropriate amount of life insurance coverage required.

name: CURRENT INCOME
description: The present earnings of an individual.

name: GOOD DEBT
description: A financial concept referring to debt that helps individuals reach goals or build assets, such as borrowing for education, a reliable car, or a home.

name: REPOSSESS (REPO)
description: The action a lender takes to seize an asset (like a car) that was used as collateral for a loan when the borrower fails to make payments.

name: SHORT-TERM LOANS
description: A category of loans, sometimes considered "bad debt," often used to cover cash flow gaps and repaid over a brief period.

name: PAWN LOANS
description: A type of loan where personal property is used as collateral, often categorized as "bad debt.

name: MONEY EMERGENCIES
description: Urgent and unexpected financial situations that require immediate attention and can be addressed through specific financial tools and strategies provided by the CFPB.

name: INCOME TRACKER
description: A tool provided by the CFPB to help users identify and monitor all sources of their income and financial resources, aiding in financial planning and understanding funds flow.

name: BEHIND ON BILLS
description: A financial state where an individual has overdue payments, leading to potential stress and financial difficulties, which is the primary problem the CFPB's guide aims to address.

name: AUTO LOAN PROVIDER
description: An organization that offers car loans and typically provides gap coverage to borrowers.

name: RENTAL CAR COSTS
description: Expenses incurred for renting a temporary vehicle, which can be covered by car rental reimbursement coverage if the insured's vehicle is being repaired after a covered claim.

name: CAR’S REPAIRS
description: The costs associated with fixing damage to one's own vehicle, which can be covered by collision or comprehensive insurance after a deductible.

name: MINIMUM COVERAGE LIMITS
description: The lowest amount of insurance coverage required by state law for various types of auto insurance, such as liability.

name: MARKET VALUE
description: The estimated worth of a vehicle in the current market, which is a factor in determining whether to maintain collision and comprehensive coverage for older cars and is relevant if a vehicle is totaled.

name: REPAIR YOUR VEHICLE
description: The action of fixing damage to one's car, which may be covered by insurance depending on the policy and type of damage.

name: SETTING ASIDE MONEY
description: The fundamental act of designating funds for future use or goals, a core component of saving.

name: TRAINING
description: Instruction or practice in a specific skill or subject, often requiring an investment of time and money.

name: ATTITUDES ABOUT MONEY
description: An individual's mindset and beliefs regarding financial management, impacting saving and spending behavior.

name: PLAN FOR THE FUTURE
description: The strategic process of preparing for upcoming events or long-term objectives, often involving financial foresight and goal setting.

name: ACHIEVING GOALS
description: The successful accomplishment of desired outcomes or objectives, often facilitated by disciplined financial planning and saving.

name: DEPOSIT CASH
description: The act of placing physical money into a financial account, like a savings account, for safekeeping and potential growth.

name: UNEXPECTED EXPENSE
description: A sudden, unplanned cost that arises without prior warning, often requiring funds from an emergency savings account.

name: TRANSFER FUNDS
description: The movement of money from one financial location or account to another, such as between different parts of a prepaid card or between bank accounts.

name: BENEFITS OF SAVINGS PLACES
description: The advantages and positive outcomes associated with choosing particular methods or locations for storing savings.

name: EXTRA HOURS AT WORK
description: Working additional time beyond regular scheduled hours, which typically results in increased income.

name: PAID BI-WEEKLY
description: A compensation schedule where an individual receives wages every two weeks, leading to occasional months with three paychecks.

name: PAYCHECKS
description: Regular payments received by an employee for work performed, representing a primary source of income.

name: EXTRA PAYCHECK
description: An additional paycheck that occurs in months when a bi-weekly payment schedule yields three payments instead of the usual two, often targeted for savings.

name: PREDICTABLE ANNUAL EXPENSES
description: Costs that occur once a year and can be anticipated, allowing for advance financial planning and saving.

name: MEETING GOALS
description: The fulfillment of personal or financial objectives, which can be supported by strategic saving, especially from sources like tax refunds.

name: PAYDAY LENDING
description: The practice of providing small, short-term, high-interest loans, typically repaid on the borrower's next payday.

name: CAR TITLE
description: The legal document proving ownership of a vehicle, which is given to a lender as collateral for an auto title loan.

name: INSTALLMENT PAYMENTS
description: Regular, periodic payments made to repay a loan over time, as an alternative to a single payment.

name: REPOSSESSION FEES
description: Additional charges imposed by a lender when repossessing a car due to non-payment of an auto title loan.

name: CONSUMER GOODS
description: Items purchased by consumers for personal use, such as furniture, electronics, and appliances, which can be acquired through rent-to-own arrangements.

name: RENT-TO-OWN COMPANY
description: A business that offers consumer goods to customers through rent-to-own agreements.

name: TRADITIONAL CREDIT CHECK
description: A process of evaluating a borrower's creditworthiness by reviewing their credit history, which is generally not performed for payday loans.

name: ELECTRONIC ACCESS
description: Permission granted by a borrower to a lender to automatically withdraw funds from their bank or credit union account for loan repayment.

name: AUTHORIZATION
description: Formal permission granted by a borrower to a lender, for example, to take money directly from their bank account.

name: RENEW
description: The act of extending the term of a loan, typically by paying an additional fee rather than the full principal and interest.

name: ROLLOVER
description: An action similar to renewing, where a borrower extends a loan's term, usually incurring another fee, when they cannot repay the full amount.

name: REPAYING
description: The act of paying back a loan, including the principal and any associated fees or interest, to the lender.

name: AUCTION
description: A public sale where goods, such as repossessed cars, are sold to the highest bidder.

name: RETURN
description: The act of giving an item back to the seller or owner, as an option for the borrower in rent-to-own arrangements.

name: FREQUENT RE-BORROWING
description: The pattern of repeatedly taking out new loans to cover existing financial obligations, a common problem with short-term, small dollar loans.

name: PAYDAY LOAN TERM
description: The specified duration for which a payday loan is granted, typically 14 days.

name: RESTRICT
description: To limit or control certain activities, such as payday lending, often by state law.

name: PROHIBIT
description: To formally forbid something, such as certain lending practices, often by state law.

name: INVESTMENT ACCOUNT
description: A type of account into which a savings plan, such as a 529 Plan, can be placed, often with special tax benefits.

name: INCOME TAX
description: A government levy on an individual's or entity's income, which may be incurred along with penalty fees if certain financial products are used for non-qualified purposes.

name: DEPOSIT FIXED AMOUNT OF MONEY
description: The action of placing a specific, predetermined sum of money into an account or financial product, such as a Certificate of Deposit.

name: WITHDRAW MONEY AFTER MATURITY
description: The action of taking out funds from a financial product, like a Certificate of Deposit, once the agreed-upon term has ended.

name: PAY PENALTY
description: The action of incurring and remitting a charge or fine due to a specific financial action, such as early withdrawal from a Certificate of Deposit.

name: NEGATIVE EQUITY
description: The financial situation where the amount owed on an asset, such as a car, is greater than its current market value, often a risk with longer loan terms.

name: TITLE FEES
description: Charges incurred for the legal transfer of a vehicle's ownership title, which are non-negotiable and part of the upfront car costs.

name: NON-NEGOTIABLE FEES
description: Mandatory charges associated with a car purchase, including state and local taxes and title fees, that cannot be altered through negotiation.

name: NEGOTIABLE FEES
description: Charges associated with a car purchase or loan, such as delivery, origination, document, and preparation fees, whose amounts can potentially be reduced through negotiation.

name: DELIVERY CHARGES
description: A negotiable fee covering the cost of transporting a new vehicle from the factory to the dealership.

name: ORIGINATION FEES
description: A negotiable fee charged by a lender for processing a new loan application.

name: DOCUMENT FEES
description: A negotiable fee charged for preparing and processing the paperwork involved in a car purchase or loan.

name: PREPARATION FEES
description: A negotiable fee charged for cleaning, inspecting, and preparing a new vehicle for customer delivery.

name: COST OF THE CAR BEFORE INTEREST
description: The sum of the car's price, additional features, services, add-ons, and all associated taxes and fees, prior to the calculation of any loan interest.

name: DOWN PAYMENT
description: An initial cash payment made by the buyer at the time of purchase, which reduces the total amount of money that needs to be financed and lowers the overall loan cost.

name: TRADE-IN VALUE
description: The monetary value assigned to a buyer's existing vehicle when exchanged for a new one, which can be applied to reduce the total amount to finance for the new purchase.

name: TOTAL AMOUNT TO FINANCE
description: The remaining balance of the car's cost after applying the down payment and trade-in value, representing the principal amount that will be borrowed through a loan.

name: LENGTH OF LOAN
description: The specified duration, typically in months or years, over which a loan is scheduled to be repaid; a longer term generally results in higher total interest paid.

name: DEALER
description: A business that sells cars and can provide information or services related to auto loans and car purchases.

name: TOTAL AMOUNT PAID ON THE LOAN
description: The cumulative sum of all monthly payments made throughout the entire term of an auto loan, encompassing both the principal borrowed and the interest accrued.

name: TOTAL COST OF YOUR PURCHASE
description: The comprehensive cost of acquiring a vehicle, encompassing the total amount paid on the loan, the down payment, and the trade-in value, reflecting the full financial outlay.

name: TOTAL INTEREST PAID OVER LIFE OF THE LOAN
description: The aggregate sum of all interest payments made from the beginning to the end of a loan's term.

name: NEGOTIATING
description: The act of discussing and bargaining with parties such as lenders or dealers to reach mutually agreeable terms, particularly concerning prices, fees, or interest rates.

name: EVALUATING REPAYMENT PLANS
description: The process of assessing and comparing different structured approaches for paying back student loans to determine which option best suits an individual's financial situation and needs.

name: MONEY CONVERSATION
description: A discussion about financial matters that the companion guides help contextualize.

name: FINANCIAL SITUATIONS
description: The unique financial circumstances that military life presents.

name: MILITARY CONSUMERS
description: A specific group of consumers within the military communities who face particular financial challenges.

name: NATIVE COMMUNITY MEMBERS
description: Individuals belonging to Native communities whose financial lives are addressed by a companion guide.

name: FINANCIAL LIVES
description: The overall financial circumstances and activities of individuals, particularly Native community members.

name: SEASONAL INCOME AND EXPENSES
description: Income and expenditure patterns that vary according to seasons, relevant for annual planning in Native communities.

name: ELDERS
description: An older population group, particularly in Native communities, who are susceptible to financial exploitation.

name: DISABILITY COMMUNITY
description: A population group consisting of people with disabilities, served by specific organizations and a CFPB guide.

name: INCOME FROM WORK
description: Earnings derived from employment, which can impact Social Security Income (SSI).

name: FINANCIALLY EMPOWER
description: The act of providing individuals with the knowledge, skills, and resources to take control of their financial lives.

name: FAMILIES OF CRIMINAL JUSTICE-INVOLVED INDIVIDUALS
description: Family members of individuals involved in the criminal justice system, who are also targeted by the reentry guide for financial empowerment.

name: FINANCIAL DECISIONMAKING
description: The process of making choices about how to manage money and other financial resources, especially for criminal justice-involved individuals.

name: TRANSITION
description: The period of moving from incarceration back into community settings, requiring preparation of identification documents.

name: RELEASE
description: The act of being set free from confinement, requiring financial and identification preparation.

name: COMMUNITY SETTINGS
description: Environments outside of incarceration, where individuals transition after release and may use reentry guides.

name: AUTOMATIC PAYMENT
description: A scheduled withdrawal of funds from a borrower's bank account initiated by a lender to cover a loan payment.

name: REPAY LOAN
description: The act of paying back a borrowed amount of money to the lender, fulfilling the financial obligation.

name: ALTERNATIVES TO SHORT-TERM LOANS
description: Various strategies and options available to individuals to avoid or replace the need for high-cost short-term borrowing, such as negotiating bills, using credit cards, or emergency savings.

name: SHORT ON CASH
description: A financial state where an individual has insufficient immediate funds to cover expenses or needs, often leading to considering borrowing options.

name: NEGOTIATE
description: To discuss terms and reach an agreement, particularly for extending payment deadlines for a bill or loan.

name: NEED
description: An essential requirement or necessity for which money might be spent or borrowed, distinguished from obligations or wants.

name: OBLIGATION
description: A financial or moral duty to do something or to make a payment, such as paying rent or a loan installment.

name: WANT
description: A desire for something that is not essential or necessary, influencing discretionary spending and borrowing decisions.

name: SPEND LESS MONEY
description: The activity of reducing expenditures and being more frugal with finances, often considered as an alternative to borrowing.

name: MONTHLY CARD PAYMENT
description: The periodic payment, typically once a month, required on a credit card balance.

name: MAXED OUT
description: The condition of a credit card account having reached its maximum credit limit, preventing further charges or cash advances.

name: LOWER-COST SHORT-TERM LOAN ALTERNATIVES
description: More affordable borrowing options for short periods, typically available from credit unions or banks, offering better terms than high-cost short-term loans.

name: BORROW FROM A FRIEND OR FAMILY MEMBER
description: The act of obtaining money from personal acquaintances, often an informal loan arrangement, presented as an alternative to formal loans.

name: EMERGENCY EXPENSE
description: An unexpected cost that arises suddenly and requires immediate payment, such as a car repair.

name: $350 (CAR REPAIR COST)
description: The specific monetary cost of the unexpected car repair used as an example in the comparative scenario.

name: COST INFORMATION
description: Data detailing the expenses, interest, and fees associated with different financial options for covering an emergency expense.

name: AMOUNT NEEDED
description: The specific sum of money required to cover an expense or to serve as the principal for a loan.

name: 15.99 PERCENT APR
description: The specific annual percentage rate applied to the credit card in the comparative example for covering an emergency expense.

name: 391 PERCENT APR
description: The specific annual percentage rate equivalent for the payday loan in the comparative example, calculated based on its fees and short term.

name: $15 FOR EVERY $100 BORROWED
description: The fee structure for the payday loan in the example scenario, meaning $15 is charged for each $100 of the principal.

name: FIXED MONTHLY PAYMENT OF $25
description: The specific, consistent amount paid each month for the credit card in the comparative example, used to calculate repayment time.

name: LOAN AMOUNT ($350)
description: The principal sum of $350 borrowed for the payday loan in the comparative example.

name: FEE ($52.50)
description: The specific $52.50 charge associated with the payday loan example for a 14-day term.

name: TIME TO REPAY
description: The duration provided or required for the borrower to fully pay back a loan or credit card balance.

name: 16 MONTHS
description: The estimated repayment duration for the credit card in the comparative example, based on a fixed monthly payment of $25.

name: 14 DAYS
description: The standard repayment duration for the payday loan in the comparative example.

name: $40 (CREDIT CARD FEES)
description: The specific total interest and fees incurred over 16 months when using the credit card in the comparative example.

name: $52.50 (PAYDAY LOAN FEE)
description: The specific fee incurred for a single 14-day payday loan in the comparative example.

name: TOTAL COST OF CAR REPAIR
description: The final overall expenditure for the car repair, factoring in the initial cost ($350) and any associated borrowing fees or interest.

name: $390 (CREDIT CARD TOTAL COST)
description: The total cost of the car repair ($350) plus credit card interest and fees ($40) when using a credit card.

name: $402.50 (PAYDAY LOAN TOTAL COST)
description: The total cost of the car repair ($350) plus payday loan fees ($52.50) if the loan is repaid on time.

name: RENEW OR ROLL OVER LOAN
description: The act of extending a loan's term, typically a payday loan, by paying only the fees and re-borrowing the principal, which significantly increases the total cost and duration of debt.

name: 10 WEEKS (DEBT DURATION)
description: The total duration of debt if a payday loan is renewed or rolled over four times, illustrating the extended impact of frequent re-borrowing.

name: $262.50 IN FEES
description: The cumulative fees incurred when a payday loan is renewed or rolled over four times, in addition to the original principal.

name: $350 (ORIGINAL BORROWED AMOUNT)
description: The initial principal sum of money borrowed in the payday loan rollover example.

name: $612.50 (TOTAL PAYDAY LOAN COST)
description: The total amount paid for a payday loan, including the original principal and accumulated fees, after multiple rollovers.

name: SPECIAL PROTECTION
description: Specific legal safeguards or benefits provided to certain groups, such as active duty servicemembers and their dependents, under laws like the Military Lending Act.

name: HOPES, WANTS, AND DREAMS
description: Future desires and aspirations that individuals hold for themselves and their family, which can be turned into specific goals through planning and financial consideration.

name: PLAN
description: A structured strategy or approach developed to achieve specific goals, often involving financial considerations and steps to turn hopes, wants, and dreams into reality.

name: HOLIDAYS
description: A recurring event for which individuals might set a short-term financial goal, such as saving money to buy gifts.

name: SMART GOAL METHOD
description: A structured approach to goal setting that ensures goals are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound, increasing their effectiveness and likelihood of accomplishment.

name: TOOLS OR EQUIPMENT FOR YOUR JOB
description: Physical items or machinery necessary for one's occupation, often considered a large purchase requiring financial planning.

name: COMPLAINT HOTLINE
description: A phone service, accessible via (855) 411-2372, provided by the CFPB for submitting complaints about financial products or services.

name: DEFAULT ON THE LOAN
description: The failure to make required payments on a loan, leading to potential consequences like foreclosure for a mortgage.

name: LOAN ISN'T REPAID AS AGREED
description: The failure to repay a loan according to the terms and conditions set, which can lead to additional fees or the loss of collateral.

name: SALARY OR WAGES
description: The regular financial compensation an employee receives from an employer for work performed.

name: SERVICE FEES
description: Charges incurred for using a financial product or service, such as those associated with payroll cards or prepaid cards.

name: ELECTRONIC ERROR
description: A technical malfunction or mistake in an electronic financial transaction that can lead to loss of funds, against which consumer protections often apply.

name: MONTHLY MAINTENANCE FEE
description: A recurring charge applied to an account or card, often a prepaid card, to cover administrative costs.

name: USAGE FEE
description: A charge applied each time a specific service or feature of a financial product, like a prepaid card, is utilized.

name: INSURED FUNDS
description: Money held in financial accounts that is protected by government agencies up to a certain limit in case the financial institution fails.

name: BANK FAILS
description: The event where a bank becomes insolvent and is unable to meet its financial obligations to its depositors and creditors, potentially leading to loss of uninsured funds.

name: INSURED DEPOSITS
description: Funds placed in a savings account that are guaranteed by a government entity, protecting the depositor in case the bank fails.

name: DEPOSIT (FOR SECURED CREDIT CARD)
description: The amount of money paid by a cardholder that acts as collateral for a secured credit card, often determining the credit limit.

name: BALANCE (CREDIT CARD)
description: The total amount of money owed on a credit card at a particular time.

name: FREQUENT TRANSACTIONS
description: Numerous and regular financial activities (deposits, withdrawals, purchases), which savings accounts are generally not intended for.

name: GET SMALL LOANS QUICKLY
description: The act of obtaining a small amount of money in a short timeframe, characteristic of pawn loans and payday loans.

name: PAID CASH
description: The act of giving physical currency as payment or in exchange for an item, as done in a pawn loan transaction.

name: CASHES A CHECK
description: The act of a lender converting a borrower's check into cash, or holding it for future deposit, as part of a payday loan process.

name: ELECTRONICALLY DEBITS THE PAYMENT
description: The act of a lender withdrawing funds from a borrower's bank account digitally to collect a loan payment, common with payday loans.

name: RENEW (A LOAN)
description: The act of extending the term of an existing loan, often by paying an additional fee, if the original loan isn't repaid as agreed.

name: DIRECTLY DEPOSITED
description: The act of transferring funds electronically into a bank account or onto a card, such as an employer depositing salary onto a payroll card.

name: LOADS (SALARY OR WAGES)
description: The act of an employer transferring salary or wages onto a payroll card for an employee.

name: USE (CARD)
description: The act of utilizing a financial card (like a payroll, prepaid, or debit card) for transactions such as purchases.

name: ELECTRONICALLY TRANSFER FUNDS
description: The act of moving money digitally from one account or person to another, a core function of peer-to-peer transfer services.

name: SEND AND RECEIVE FUNDS
description: The dual action of initiating and accepting money transfers, typically using a mobile device or computer for services like peer-to-peer transfers.

name: KEEP MONEY (SECURELY)
description: The act of storing funds in a safe manner, often in a secure place like a prepaid card or savings account.

name: PAY AND MANAGE BILLS
description: The act of settling financial obligations and overseeing their payment schedule, which can be done with a prepaid card.

name: SET ASIDE FUNDS
description: The act of designating money for a specific future purpose, often facilitated by prepaid cards or savings accounts.

name: ACCESS MONEY
description: The act of retrieving or utilizing funds available in an account or on a card, such as from a prepaid card.

name: PAID OR LOADED IN ADVANCE
description: The act of depositing funds into a card or account before use, characteristic of prepaid cards.

name: TAKE OUT MONEY FROM AN ATM
description: The act of withdrawing cash from an Automated Teller Machine, a function available with some prepaid cards.

name: RELOAD MONEY ONTO THE CARD
description: The act of adding more funds to an existing prepaid card.

name: SAVE FOR YOUR GOALS
description: The act of accumulating money over time to achieve specific financial objectives, supported by savings wallet features or savings accounts.

name: DEPOSIT AND WITHDRAW MONEY
description: The basic actions of placing funds into and taking funds out of a financial account, fundamental to a savings account.

name: EARNS INTEREST
description: The action of an account or investment generating additional money over time, a characteristic of savings accounts.

name: BUILD UP MONEY FOR SAVINGS OR EMERGENCIES
description: The act of accumulating funds specifically for future savings or unexpected urgent needs, typically done with a savings account.

name: BUILD A CREDIT HISTORY
description: The act of establishing and improving a record of borrowing and repayment, often accomplished using a secured credit card.

name: ASPE.HHS.GOV/POVERTY-GUIDELINES
description: A website providing the current Federal Poverty Guidelines, which are used in calculating discretionary income for income-driven repayment plans.

name: COMMUNITY RESOURCE
description: An appropriate local organization or service that provides additional financial help or guidance, to which the toolkit can refer individuals when needed.

name: HIGHEST INTEREST RATE STRATEGY
description: A debt repayment approach where individuals prioritize paying off debts with the highest interest rates first to minimize total interest paid over time.>

name: OVERTIME INCOME
description: Additional earnings received for working hours beyond the standard workweek, increasing disposable income.>

name: SECOND JOB INCOME
description: Earnings from an additional employment position, contributing to overall income for expenses or debt repayment.>

name: SEASONAL SNAPSHOT TOOL
description: A financial planning tool or method to analyze and understand how personal expenses fluctuate throughout the year, aiding in preparation for variable costs.>

name: HOLIDAY GIFTS
description: An example of a common seasonal expense that requires financial planning.>

name: HEATING COSTS
description: An example of a variable or seasonal utility expense.>

name: HOME REPAIRS
description: An example of an unexpected or variable expense related to property maintenance.>

name: TAX PAYMENTS
description: Mandatory financial contributions to government revenue, which are often planned expenses.>

name: SUMMER ACTIVITIES COSTS
description: An example of seasonal expenses related to recreational activities during summer months.>

name: CAR INSURANCE BILL
description: A recurring financial obligation for vehicle insurance coverage.>

name: BACK-TO-SCHOOL SUPPLIES
description: An example of a seasonal expense incurred for educational purposes.>

name: NEW CLOTHES
description: An example of a variable expense for personal apparel.>

name: PRIORITIZING DEBT PAYMENTS STRATEGY
